emails.subject: EYP 2025 – Monday Recap + Tuesday Schedule
emails.body:
Dear Karthik,

Thank you for joining us today for the first day of *Engineering Your Ph.D. (EYP) 2025*! We hope the sessions gave you a strong start to your doctoral journey and introduced you to the people and resources that will support you along the way.

As promised, attached are PDF versions of today's presentations for your reference.

*Tomorrow's Schedule – Tuesday, August 26*

*Location: Carleton Commons (Mudd, 4th floor)*

   - 11:00 – 11:15 AM – Welcome and Brief Overview
   - 11:15 – 12:15 PM – CTL: Essentials of Teaching
   - 12:15 – 1:00 PM – Lunch
   - 1:00 – 2:00 PM – SEAS TA Panel
   - 2:00 – 3:00 PM – Inclusion and Belonging

*Looking Ahead to Thursday*

   -

   We will close EYP with a *Toast Reception* on *Thursday, August 28, from 1:00 – 3:00 PM in Carleton Commons, *a chance to gather together and celebrate the start of your Ph.D. journey.
   -

   In addition, we are planning a *Thursday evening social event*. More details will follow soon. Stay tuned!

We look forward to seeing you back tomorrow as we continue with sessions on teaching, the TA experience, and building a sense of belonging within the Columbia Engineering community.

Best regards,
*Richard Hagen, Ph.D.* *(He/Him)*
Assistant Director, Graduate Student Affairs
Engineering Student Affairs
Columbia Engineering
emails.snippet: Thank you for joining us today for the first day of Engineering Your Ph.D. (EYP) 2025!

emails.subject: Welcome New CS PhD Students
emails.body:
Dear New PhD Students,

I am the Director of IT for the Department of Computer Science. The CS IT team is Computing Research Facilities (CRF) and we will be working with you during your time here.

We can be reached at crf@cs.columbia.edu.

Please read over the following items, especially the first one regarding CS account creation.

1. Your Computer Science Account

We need to create your CS account. Please note that if you were here previously and already have an account with the same name as your UNI, you should still apply for a new one and we will transfer your home directory over.

Your account name can be whatever you like as long as it is available.

I'd like to encourage you to choose an account name that is both readable and professional, keeping in mind that it will also be used for your CS email (see item 2). You can use your name (such as nancysmith or nsmith) as long as it is not already in use.

Account request page: https://www.cs.columbia.edu/crf/account-form/
emails.snippet: Welcome to the Computer Science PhD program at Columbia University!

emails.subject: Food Bank For New York City Opportunity Sign-Up Confirmation
emails.body:
Dear Karthik Sreedhar,

Thank you for registering to participate in Mobile Pantry @Chinatown YMCA (MANHATTAN) from 9/12/2025, 10:00 AM until 9/12/2025, 2:00 PM. We are excited to have you volunteer with us!

LOCATION: Chinatown Branch YMCA
100 Hester Street New York NY, United States 10002

WHAT TO WEAR:
Volunteers are expected to present a neat appearance and dress according to the requirements of the volunteer shift.

We ask that volunteers do not wear revealing attire such as muscle shirts, crop tops, "short shorts" or see-through clothing. Any shorts or skirts worn must be below the knee.

Sturdy, closed-toe, closed-heel shoes, such as athletic sneakers or boots, are required for safety. Ballet flats, loafers, slides, sandals, flip flops, crocs, and high-heels are not allowed.

PLEASE NOTE:
All volunteers must agree to Food Bank for New York City's volunteer waivers in order to participate.

Volunteers are expected to arrive on-time and stay for the duration of the project. If for some reason you are no longer able to attend, please let us know as soon as possible, so another volunteer can take your place.
emails.snippet: Thank you for signing up to volunteer with Food Bank For New York City!

emails.subject: Welcome to Fall 2025 from EGSC - DR Applications Open Now!
emails.body:
Dear Graduate SEAS,

Welcome all new and returning students to the Fall 2025 semester!

For those who may not know us yet, the Engineering Graduate Student Council
(EGSC) is a student-run organization dedicated to:

   -

   Serving as your voice for student concerns
   -

   Driving positive change within our community
   -

   Hosting social events to help you unwind and connect
   -

   Fostering a sense of community among our graduate students, both within
   Columbia and across NYC

I’m excited to introduce you to the Executive Board
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/content/executive-board-2025  >!

[image: image.png]

Make sure you connect with us on Slack
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__cuegsc.slack.com_ssb_redirect&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=ul8QkDqRh_NYIgxWcCfEU6pkVKR221Kvf0vUqobyKbJhd1zj0iTCniRzauKT4TdE&m=WLP3oLJBrCHGS29Bv1pi6z-78Bu3v3MmTZb7sC-Q8bDQShOVqn5AB024plTqT28L&s=nRxLel2EjC6HI93OpMjdH2VPWEyK1PV7VIuDH66Em5o&e= >, Instagram
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.instagram.com_columbiaegsc&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=ul8QkDqRh_NYIgxWcCfEU6pkVKR221Kvf0vUqobyKbJhd1zj0iTCniRzauKT4TdE&m=WLP3oLJBrCHGS29Bv1pi6z-78Bu3v3MmTZb7sC-Q8bDQShOVqn5AB024plTqT28L&s=gNAornxXObj3zrfk0YYR2A-2f_wvFoVsUCKEwAzO8Bs&e= >, and look out for our weekly
newsletter every Tuesday from our Communications Chair, Jonovan (you should
have gotten one already yesterday)!

We're thrilled to represent you and have an action-packed semester ahead!
Get ready for:

   -

   Social events (on-campus, off-campus, and with schools across Columbia
   and NYC) 🎊
   -

   Alumni chats and panels 💬
   -

   Professional Development Scholarships 🎓
   -

   A boat cruise ⛴️
   -

   And much more!

Want to join us on EGSC? Become a Department Representative (DR)! APPLY NOW
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__forms.gle_jv6y8DXcdoqLpVXcA&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=ul8QkDqRh_NYIgxWcCfEU6pkVKR221Kvf0vUqobyKbJhd1zj0iTCniRzauKT4TdE&m=WLP3oLJBrCHGS29Bv1pi6z-78Bu3v3MmTZb7sC-Q8bDQShOVqn5AB024plTqT28L&s=A8GPbggf1SMxonaDJ0bK7GuN3waqPwkxWpQuZmVO7e8&e= >!

As a DR, you'll:

   -

   Be the liaison between your department and EGSC
   -

   Bring student ideas and concerns to the table
   -

   Help shape EGSC events by joining one of our committees
   <https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/content/committees  >

How to apply:

   -

   Applications are open until 11:59 PM, Friday, September 12th
   -

   Elections will be held the following week (September 15th & 16th)
   -

   Grad students in each department/program will vote on their department’s
   candidates
   -

   More details in the application form: APPLY HERE
   <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__forms.gle_jv6y8DXcdoqLpVXcA&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=ul8QkDqRh_NYIgxWcCfEU6pkVKR221Kvf0vUqobyKbJhd1zj0iTCniRzauKT4TdE&m=WLP3oLJBrCHGS29Bv1pi6z-78Bu3v3MmTZb7sC-Q8bDQShOVqn5AB024plTqT28L&s=A8GPbggf1SMxonaDJ0bK7GuN3waqPwkxWpQuZmVO7e8&e= >

[image: image.png]

Can't commit to being a DR? No problem! You're still welcome to join our
Friday meetings (4:00 - 5:00 PM, starting September 19th) and share your
thoughts!

If you have any questions, concerns, or ideas for SEAS, please don't
hesitate to reach out to:

   -

   President Thomas Bina (president@egsc.seas.columbia.edu)
   -

   Vice President Jenny Gao (vp@egsc.seas.columbia.edu)
   -

   Or any other E-Board member

Best wishes,
-- 

*Thomas Bina*
President
Engineering Graduate Student Council <http://egsc.seas.columbia.edu/  >
*Columbia University *
president@egsc.seas.columbia.edu
He/Him/His

emails.subject: Appy to be an Ambassador!
emails.body:
Columbia | Engineering.

Hello Karthik,                                              The Office of Graduate Admissions at Columbia Engineering is searching for volunteer Graduate Student Ambassadors!                       

                        We are looking for outgoing and enthusiastic current students who are excited to talk to prospective and admitted students about their Columba experience.                       

                        We aim to have ambassadors from all academic departments to represent our wide range of program offerings.                      

Hours: Expected 1-2 events a semester. While most events are to be fulfilled in person during the 9 am-5 pm business day, there will be opportunities for virtual evening and weekend hours.

                        Tasks include:                      

	                             Welcoming visitors to the Graduate Admissions Office & On-Campus events                          

	                             Leading campus tours (Mostly in-person but may be offered virtually)                          

	                             Attending and speaking at prospective and admitted student events (mainly as a panelist)                          

	                             Attending required trainings to become familiar with the programs offered at Columbia Engineering                          

	                             Work with the Admissions team to generate external content for blogs, emails, and social media posts                          

                        Benefits:                      

	                             Network! Meet a variety of people, including fellow students, prospective students, staff, faculty, and administration                          

	                             Experience! This role offers a transferable and resume-building experience                          

                        To Apply:                      

	                             Complete Online Application Form <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__forms.gle_xX3pSymmubmAcyTu5&d=DwIFAg&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=FsxirmszmvXtNjiwnN9WlYTU2gMTZGPDztgPy1iX2MifMugPB3k_ea-Qzx-bXw8R&s=fYQs38clx2EW0pCoFLgLNedgcMMLNONuWX-Ur6saiWY&e= >

                        Deadline:                      

	 Wednesday, September 17th, 2025

                        We will review applications and follow up with selected candidates for a brief interview. If you have any questions or would like more information about the position, please feel free to reach out to Ms. Natasha Stanislas at nzs2103@columbia.edu.                      

                        Thank you for considering this unique opportunity to contribute to our vibrant community. We look forward to welcoming you as a valued Student Ambassador at Columbia Engineering Graduate Admissions.                      

                      Best,

                      Gabrielle Gannon
                      Associate Dean of Graduate Admissions
                      Office of Graduate Admissions
                      The Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science
                      Columbia University
Office of Graduate Admissions
                      500 W. 120th Street
                      New York, NY 10027

                      Admissions
                      (212) 854-4688
seasgradmit@columbia.edu

>>     the PhD. Would sincerely appreciate some guidance with
>> this, appreciate
>>     your help!

>>     Thanks,
>>     Karthik

>>     On Thu, Aug 7, 2025 at 1:29â€¯PM PhD Student Services
>> <
>>     phd-advising@cs.columbia.edu> wrote:

>>         Hi everyone,

>>         As a reminder, fall 2025 registration is open. I
>> am sending more
>>         personal registration reminders to smaller groups
>> of students and
>>         individuals as I review fall 2025 registration.
>> Please keep an eye on
>>         your email and do the required action as soon as
>> possible.

>>         The Student Health Insurance plan is set to expire
>> on August 14, 2025
>>         if you are not enrolled for fall 2025. You will
>> also not appear on the
>>         Finance Team's fall 2025 hiring/funding roster!
>> They cannot complete
>>         their tasks if you are not enrolled in fall 2025.

>>         Thank you,

emails.subject: Climate Week at Columbia Engineering!
emails.body:
View this email in your browser
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__us9.campaign-2Darchive.com_-3Fe-3D-5F-5Ftest-5Femail-5F-5F-26u-3Ddf27932934a60ad09efdd5a4f-26id-3Dabae2e719e&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=IldiK8XidMOdHX62LG8nFWqQiR7dDww4PcxZ6lZOm1k&m=aRn56F-1J-uLZ4_0Ro3mWGLCcNdqDg01k3lvZ-mDdTI62oyvdeW5LqXDoR_Tztx6&s=Lrxb1dVdRbEA77jzw_tR0_xPbUQ-2_0Ahj-l87Sag0U&e=>
<https://www.engineering.columbia.edu/about/events/climate-week-columbia-engineering-2025?utm_source=Columbia+Engineering+Communications&utm_campaign=abae2e719e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_08_20_02_46_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-dc2af10a51-  >
Announcing Climate Week 2025 at Columbia Engineering

Climate Week at Columbia Engineering is just around the corner. The week of
September 22 will be dedicated to events and talks from leading researchers
and experts who are at the forefront of developing solutions to help the
planet and society. We are joining with others across New York City to
advance climate action as part of Climate Week NYC.

In collaboration with partners in and outside of Columbia, we have a full
roster of events, including the launch of a new Columbia-led NYCEDC
materials innovation hub--Gotham Foundry.

Please see the Climate Week website
<https://www.engineering.columbia.edu/about/events/climate-week-columbia-engineering-2025?utm_source=Columbia+Engineering+Communications&utm_campaign=abae2e719e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_08_20_02_46_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-dc2af10a51-  >
for the schedule and event locations.

I hope you can join us for these events!

Sincerely,

Shih-Fu Chang

Dean, Columbia Engineering

*CLIMATE WEEK AT COLUMBIA ENGINEERING September 22-26, 2025 *
*MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 22 | 10-11:30AM*
*Tech CEO Lecture - Alexander Sarrigeorgiou, Eurolife FFH, '79SEAS,
'80SEAS: "Navigating Climate Challenges: Resilience through Insurance,
Markets and Public Policy"
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.eventbrite.com_e_1478627838669-3Faff-3Doddtdtcreator-26utm-5Fsource-3DColumbia-2BEngineering-2BCommunications-26utm-5Fcampaign-3Dabae2e719e-2DEMAIL-5FCAMPAIGN-5F2024-5F08-5F20-5F02-5F46-5FCOPY-5F01-26utm-5Fmedium-3Demail-26utm-5Fterm-3D0-5F-2Ddc2af10a51-2D&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=IldiK8XidMOdHX62LG8nFWqQiR7dDww4PcxZ6lZOm1k&m=aRn56F-1J-uLZ4_0Ro3mWGLCcNdqDg01k3lvZ-mDdTI62oyvdeW5LqXDoR_Tztx6&s=bEtM9ZJuQ6Mf3QSlmTxUurOyKaHhtWO7saW0AF9Sgv0&e=>*

Climate disasters are worsening, but less than 25% of losses are typically
insured, forcing households and governments to bear rising recovery costs
caused by weak regulations, high costs, and limited market support. To
build climate resilience, we must align regulation, public policy, and
insurance markets to shift from reactive aid to proactive risk management.
This special lecture will be co-hosted by Columbia Engineering, the Tamer
Institute for Social Enterprise and Climate Change at Columbia Business
School
<https://business.columbia.edu/socialenterprise?utm_source=Columbia+Engineering+Communications&utm_campaign=abae2e719e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_08_20_02_46_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-dc2af10a51-  >,
and Columbia Climate School
<https://www.climate.columbia.edu/?utm_source=Columbia+Engineering+Communications&utm_campaign=abae2e719e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_08_20_02_46_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-dc2af10a51-  >
.

*MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 22 | 1-3:30PM*
*Gotham Foundry Kick-Off
<engineeringevents@columbia.edu?subject=Information%20Request%3A%20Gotham%20Foundry%20Kick-Off%20Event&body=Hello%2C%0A%0AI%20am%20interested%20in%20finding%20out%20more%20about%20the%20kick-off%20event%20during%20Climate%20Week%20at%20Columbia%20Engineering.>*

Gotham Foundry is a new Materials Hub in Harlem advancing regenerative and
biodegradable material innovation while training the next-generation green
workforce to position NYC as a leader in the circular materials economy. In
partnership with major academic institutions and community organizations,
it offers cutting-edge research, shared infrastructure, and access for
deep-tech entrepreneurs to transform legacy industries like fashion,
construction, and food. Hosted by Columbia Engineering, FIT, CUNY-ASRC,
NYCEDC, and Genspace.

*MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 22 | 3:30-5:30PM*
*Data and Partnerships for Global Clean Air and Climate
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.eventbrite.com_e_data-2Dand-2Dpartnerships-2Dfor-2Dglobal-2Dclean-2Dair-2Dand-2Dclimate-2Dtickets-2D1501650710699-3Faff-3Doddtdtcreator-26utm-5Fsource-3DColumbia-2BEngineering-2BCommunications-26utm-5Fcampaign-3Dabae2e719e-2DEMAIL-5FCAMPAIGN-5F2024-5F08-5F20-5F02-5F46-5FCOPY-5F01-26utm-5Fmedium-3Demail-26utm-5Fterm-3D0-5F-2Ddc2af10a51-2D&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=IldiK8XidMOdHX62LG8nFWqQiR7dDww4PcxZ6lZOm1k&m=aRn56F-1J-uLZ4_0Ro3mWGLCcNdqDg01k3lvZ-mDdTI62oyvdeW5LqXDoR_Tztx6&s=sX64td1570W4hAic1WTELjbqGafVDZntJb15osTvr9c&e=>*

Air pollution is a global public health crisis, causing more than 8 million
premature deaths annually. The environmental damage of air pollution must
be reversed hand in hand with addressing the climate challenge. Sensing,
data science, and sustainable energy technology all help remove barriers
towards progress towards cleaner air and sustainable climate. For real
impact, this work must be co-designed and performed on a local level in
partnership with those who are most affected. We will highlight creative
and innovative work at the interface of environmental science, engineering,
public health, data science, economics, policy, and community engagement.
Hosted by Columbia Engineering and Clean Air Toolbox for Cities
<https://csud.climate.columbia.edu/content/clean-air-toolbox-cities?utm_source=Columbia+Engineering+Communications&utm_campaign=abae2e719e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_08_20_02_46_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-dc2af10a51-  >
.

*WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 24 | 8:30AM-7PM*
*CEEC Fall Symposium
<https://ceec.engineering.columbia.edu/events/2025-ceec-fall-symposium?utm_source=Columbia+Engineering+Communications&utm_campaign=abae2e719e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_08_20_02_46_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-dc2af10a51-  >:
Climate Solutions for Sustainable Energy, Chemicals, and Materials
<https://ceec.engineering.columbia.edu/events/2025-ceec-fall-symposium-registration?utm_source=Columbia+Engineering+Communications&utm_campaign=abae2e719e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_08_20_02_46_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-dc2af10a51-  >*

The Fall Symposium for the Columbia Electrochemical Engineering Center
<https://ceec.engineering.columbia.edu/?utm_source=Columbia+Engineering+Communications&utm_campaign=abae2e719e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_08_20_02_46_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-dc2af10a51-  >
will
engage attendees with three session topics: 1) Scalable electrochemical
tech in real world settings; 2) Optimal operation of electrochemical
devices; and 3) Critical materials: reducing dependence and securing supply
chains. Keynote talks on each topic will look toward future challenges,
opportunities, and emerging trends, and will be followed by panel
discussions that delve into the technical barriers to large scale
deployment. Hosted by Columbia Engineering and the Columbia Electrochemical
Engineering Center.

*WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 24 | 2-6PM*
*The Race to Fusion Energy: International Cooperation and Competition
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.eventbrite.com_e_1478668129179-3Faff-3Doddtdtcreator-26utm-5Fsource-3DColumbia-2BEngineering-2BCommunications-26utm-5Fcampaign-3Dabae2e719e-2DEMAIL-5FCAMPAIGN-5F2024-5F08-5F20-5F02-5F46-5FCOPY-5F01-26utm-5Fmedium-3Demail-26utm-5Fterm-3D0-5F-2Ddc2af10a51-2D&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=IldiK8XidMOdHX62LG8nFWqQiR7dDww4PcxZ6lZOm1k&m=aRn56F-1J-uLZ4_0Ro3mWGLCcNdqDg01k3lvZ-mDdTI62oyvdeW5LqXDoR_Tztx6&s=ANy3o4unc2s5469x8lQganv25IbcL-dzJwM4jysnLC4&e=>*

As fusion energy nears commercialization, countries are racing to develop
it through varying technical paths and public-private partnerships,
balancing global cooperation and intense competition. This event will
explore that dynamic landscape through a panel with international energy
policy leaders, followed by a reception and lab tours. Hosted by Columbia
Engineering, the Columbia Fusion Research Center
<https://fusion.columbia.edu/?utm_source=Columbia+Engineering+Communications&utm_campaign=abae2e719e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_08_20_02_46_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-dc2af10a51-  >,
and the Fusion Industry Association.

*THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 25 | 8AM-7:30PM*
*Rethinking Water
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.eventcreate.com_e_rethinkingwater2025-3Futm-5Fsource-3DColumbia-2BEngineering-2BCommunications-26utm-5Fcampaign-3Dabae2e719e-2DEMAIL-5FCAMPAIGN-5F2024-5F08-5F20-5F02-5F46-5FCOPY-5F01-26utm-5Fmedium-3Demail-26utm-5Fterm-3D0-5F-2Ddc2af10a51-2D&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=IldiK8XidMOdHX62LG8nFWqQiR7dDww4PcxZ6lZOm1k&m=aRn56F-1J-uLZ4_0Ro3mWGLCcNdqDg01k3lvZ-mDdTI62oyvdeW5LqXDoR_Tztx6&s=McOtWUjdwGD5fe2-aWJVcnLDcOq3ZfD-9W3Iewcm0D0&e=>
2025
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.eventcreate.com_e_rethinkingwater2025-3Futm-5Fsource-3DColumbia-2BEngineering-2BCommunications-26utm-5Fcampaign-3Dabae2e719e-2DEMAIL-5FCAMPAIGN-5F2024-5F08-5F20-5F02-5F46-5FCOPY-5F01-26utm-5Fmedium-3Demail-26utm-5Fterm-3D0-5F-2Ddc2af10a51-2D&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=IldiK8XidMOdHX62LG8nFWqQiR7dDww4PcxZ6lZOm1k&m=aRn56F-1J-uLZ4_0Ro3mWGLCcNdqDg01k3lvZ-mDdTI62oyvdeW5LqXDoR_Tztx6&s=McOtWUjdwGD5fe2-aWJVcnLDcOq3ZfD-9W3Iewcm0D0&e=>*

The 6th annual Rethinking Water Conference is the premier water-focused
event during New York Climate Week. It brings together leaders from
finance, academia, government, and industry to develop practical solutions
to the most urgent water challenges facing the United States. Hosted by
Sciens Water in collaboration with the Columbia University Water Center
<https://water.columbia.edu/?utm_source=Columbia+Engineering+Communications&utm_campaign=abae2e719e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_08_20_02_46_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-dc2af10a51-  >,
Columbia Engineering, and the Water Institute at Arizona State University.

*THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 25 | 11AM-2PM*
*GenAI for Climate Science
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.eventbrite.com_e_1478678801099-3Faff-3Doddtdtcreator-26utm-5Fsource-3DColumbia-2BEngineering-2BCommunications-26utm-5Fcampaign-3Dabae2e719e-2DEMAIL-5FCAMPAIGN-5F2024-5F08-5F20-5F02-5F46-5FCOPY-5F01-26utm-5Fmedium-3Demail-26utm-5Fterm-3D0-5F-2Ddc2af10a51-2D&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=IldiK8XidMOdHX62LG8nFWqQiR7dDww4PcxZ6lZOm1k&m=aRn56F-1J-uLZ4_0Ro3mWGLCcNdqDg01k3lvZ-mDdTI62oyvdeW5LqXDoR_Tztx6&s=j4SIp4eDwF8fJbCCxsZBCP0eaxX6BJ0tVCryl4IoX9g&e=>*

The NSF
<https://leap.columbia.edu/?utm_source=Columbia+Engineering+Communications&utm_campaign=abae2e719e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_08_20_02_46_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-dc2af10a51-  >LEAP
Science and Technology Center
<https://leap.columbia.edu/?utm_source=Columbia+Engineering+Communications&utm_campaign=abae2e719e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_08_20_02_46_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-dc2af10a51-  >
invites you to a panel on how Generative AI is transforming climate
science, from modeling and prediction to real-time decision support.
Featuring experts from academia, industry, and tech, the discussion will
cover current applications, challenges, and future directions, followed by
a networking lunch. Hosted by Columbia Engineering and Learning the Earth
with Artificial Intelligence and Physics (LEAP) NSF Science and Technology
Center.

*FRIDAY, SEPTEMBER 26 | 8:30AM-7PM*
*Columbia Center of Advanced Electrification Fall Symposium
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.eventbrite.com_e_1474358890129-3Faff-3Doddtdtcreator-26utm-5Fsource-3DColumbia-2BEngineering-2BCommunications-26utm-5Fcampaign-3Dabae2e719e-2DEMAIL-5FCAMPAIGN-5F2024-5F08-5F20-5F02-5F46-5FCOPY-5F01-26utm-5Fmedium-3Demail-26utm-5Fterm-3D0-5F-2Ddc2af10a51-2D&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=IldiK8XidMOdHX62LG8nFWqQiR7dDww4PcxZ6lZOm1k&m=aRn56F-1J-uLZ4_0Ro3mWGLCcNdqDg01k3lvZ-mDdTI62oyvdeW5LqXDoR_Tztx6&s=3Aa_bSj_C1iX900OjPB3pxNyTpP_xNpDuMjf8LONB70&e=>*

The inaugural Columbia Center of Advanced Electrification (CCAE)
<https://ccae.columbia.edu/?utm_source=Columbia+Engineering+Communications&utm_campaign=abae2e719e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_08_20_02_46_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-dc2af10a51-  >
Fall
Symposium will explore the forefront of electrification and energy systems
through three sessions: Power and Energy Converters and Control,
Decentralized Grids, and Transportation and Building Electrification. Each
session will feature discussions on emerging trends, opportunities, and
challenges in the field. Panel discussions will delve into technical
barriers and potential solutions for widespread adoption. Throughout the
day, a poster session featuring work by CCAE PhD students and postdocs will
offer attendees deeper insights into ongoing projects and innovations.
Hosted by Columbia Engineering and CCAE.
[image: Twitter]
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__x.com_CUSEAS-3Futm-5Fsource-3DColumbia-2BEngineering-2BCommunications-26utm-5Fcampaign-3Dabae2e719e-2DEMAIL-5FCAMPAIGN-5F2024-5F08-5F20-5F02-5F46-5FCOPY-5F01-26utm-5Fmedium-3Demail-26utm-5Fterm-3D0-5F-2Ddc2af10a51-2D&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=IldiK8XidMOdHX62LG8nFWqQiR7dDww4PcxZ6lZOm1k&m=aRn56F-1J-uLZ4_0Ro3mWGLCcNdqDg01k3lvZ-mDdTI62oyvdeW5LqXDoR_Tztx6&s=vZdi2MF2NDMxmjN4vqYiHgdboXPk8Pv6Kc6AA9960BM&e=>

emails.subject: Survey on gender-specific challenges of investigative journalism
emails.body:
Hello,

My name is Katrina Michalak, and I am a fourth-year undergraduate student
at the Walter Cronkite School of Journalism and Mass Communication at
Arizona State University, working under the guidance of Professor Maud
Beelman. I am conducting research for my honors thesis, which seeks to
identify gender-specific challenges journalists face in investigative
reporting.

I am hoping that you will help me investigate this important topic. With
IRE’s permission, I have accessed the email addresses of members as listed
on ire.org.

Your participation in this survey is voluntary. It should take only about
15 minutes to answer the series of questions, and you must be 18 or older
to participate.

To protect your confidentiality, this survey will not record any names or
personally identifying information, such as email addresses. Your responses
will be anonymous, unless you choose to provide your name and contact
information in an optional, open-ended text box at the end of the survey.

The results of my research may be used in reports, presentations or
publications, but you will not be identified in any way unless you have
given permission. I may reach out using your voluntary contact information
to ask follow-up questions.

Although there is no direct benefit to you, there are possible benefits to
the journalism field. You will be contributing to unique research in the
profession and raising awareness on various challenges journalists in
investigative reporting face. Our questionnaire has been reviewed by ASU’s
Institutional Review Board, which requires me to note that there are no
foreseeable risks to your participation.

If you do choose to take this survey – and I really hope you will – please
do so by Saturday, September 13, at 11:59pm EST.

If you have any questions about the study, please contact the research team
at kmichal3@asu.edu or Maud.Beelman@asu.edu. If you have any questions
about your rights as a subject/participant in this research, or if you feel
you have been placed at risk, you can contact the Chair of the Human
Subjects Institutional Review Board, through the ASU Office of Research
Integrity and Assurance, at (480) 965-6788.

Take the survey here.
<https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScpIksYEKvh1vGscbOsfHVkdLzAK-Tpsgib-tXQwnZvWgbR_w/viewform >

-- 
*Katrina Michalak, she/her *
*978.435.5849*
The State Press
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.statepress.com/__;!!IKRxdwAv5BmarQ!bfKiYDs4DGtUJVWzmnYdOgSfsScgQs7vEHtO5QyQjPRQ0do2cEPzIyhlvvq8vE0npDMjvp18HLnmnNuh5Bx6hk447WM$ >
Executive
Editor
ASU Walter Cronkite School of Journalism and Mass Communication
Barrett, The Honors College Scholar

emails.subject: New York Anti-Sexual Harassment Training 2024-2025 - Due in 5 Days
emails.body:
Dear Karthik Sreedhar:
This is a reminder that you have 5 days to complete the New York Anti-Sexual Harassment Training 2024-2025 training course.
This annual course is mandated by New York State and New York City law, and all Faculty, Staff and Contractors at the University are required to complete it every year. As a reminder, because the content is specific to Columbia University, you are required to complete the course whether or not you have taken similar courses elsewhere. This course will take approximately 45-60 minutes to complete.
Training must be completed by 10-SEP-2025.
To complete the course, click https://columbia.sabacloud.com/Saba/Web_spf/NA3P1PRD0112/common/ledetail/cours000000000055814 Login with your UNI and password and click 'LAUNCH' to start the course. 
If you prefer not to click on the above link, you may also access the training by going directly to ELM (the University’s Enterprise Learning Management system) at columbia.sabacloud.com, logging in with your UNI and password, and then clicking on “My Learning Plan,” and locating New York Anti-Sexual Harassment Training 2024-2025.
The Office of Institutional Equity recommends completing the course in one sitting to avoid the system timing out and requiring you to restart the course from the beginning.
If you have any questions, please send an email to OIEtraining@columbia.edu.
For technology-related issues, contact askcuit@columbia.edu or call the CUIT Service Desk at (212) 854-1919.
Thank you for your cooperation. 
 
The Office of Institutional Equity
Columbia University

Dear Student,

Students need to confirm enrollment or request a waiver for the 2025-2026 plan year.

YOU MUST TAKE ACTION REGARDING HEALTH INSURANCE EVERY YEAR.

** What you need to do:
------------------------------------------------------------
Confirm your enrollment in the Columbia Plan (https://www.health.columbia.edu/content/how-enroll-columbia-student-health-insurance-plan )
Read about the Columbia Plan's benefits. (https://www.health.columbia.edu/content/about-columbia-university-student-health-insurance-plan )

OR
Submit a waiver request (https://secure.health.columbia.edu/ )
If you’ve already submitted your waiver request,
please allow 5-7 business days for processing.
You can check the status of your request on the Patient Portal.

** Optional:
------------------------------------------------------------
Enrollment in the voluntary Dependent, Dental or Vision plans must be completed each plan year. Enrollment does NOT automatically continue.
Enroll dependents (https://www.health.columbia.edu/content/insurance-families )
Enroll in the voluntary Dental or Vision insurance plans (https://www.health.columbia.edu/content/voluntary-dental-and-vision-plans )

The deadline to enroll or submit a waiver is September 30, 2025

Questions?
Email the Student Health Insurance Office studentinsurance@columbia.edu (mailto:studentinsurance@columbia.edu) .

Be well,

Student Health Insurance Office

============================================================

emails.subject: Support for applications to NSF GRFP - Registration Info
emails.body:
Dear Students,

Welcome to the new academic year. We write to remind you that the Graduate
School of Arts and Sciences (GSAS) and the School of Engineering and
Applied Science (SEAS) are hosting a series of events for students applying
for the National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellowship Program
(NSF GRFP)
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__mx.technolutions.net_ss_c_u001.jxaZRujEt1lhE0WEp4QewHHx-2DoTDKdO6LxFACnBIQwY_4ir_q76-2DKDpVTEaR0gJCBrr2iQ_h0_h001.SRXNKdBlMFhw6gGCp-5F6hPgjwdJEm-2DAxiMOcw5pLY1rs&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=T_qWyn748-1L3J6iCsnAvCLqb1OOQPXhQW53UE3dWJM&m=TSXpxIg6fZ3Mutw4nwqA7_rT1iWDFnH2-Its7V4v1uAcydfeWqGDc-11u7Vup63o&s=bLYuzq5KxK3nY9f8istkYOErbGUc6Kka2egFTvvLdHY&e=>
.

We encourage you to attend these presentations and workshops designed to
guide you through the application process, and to work one-on-one with
writing consultants. Each event will be recorded and made available to
registrants. Despite some uncertainty about the dates for the GRFP
competition, we think it is important for you to be prepared.

ONLINE EVENTS:

NSF Graduate Fellowship Presentation

Friday, September 19, 12:30–2:30 pm (Zoom Meeting Link
<https://columbiauniversity.zoom.us/meeting/register/lOqNm1FgRJCvXdutXsEiJg >
)

This online presentation will provide information about preparing a
competitive NSF fellowship application. Professors Helen H. Lu and Ruben
Gonzalez, who have served as NSF fellowship reviewers, will offer tips. A
panel of NSF fellows will share their experiences and advice. Register here
<https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1jDwgiykKupl-lFgfMfAtvDeZtQ11kVMnlTq3E1yna08/edit >
.

Writing a Winning NSF GRFP Application

Monday, September 22, 3:00–5:00 pm (Zoom Meeting Link
<https://columbiauniversity.zoom.us/meeting/register/CenFEbyrQy2gZ15Xx7NB3A >
)

This online workshop will focus on GRFP application essays (personal
statement and research proposal). Students will leave with actionable
insights and strategies for writing their own essays. Register here
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__forms.gle_NoyoQQUcKH5Pizqp8&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=57l3KMEuhT5lKBsgb6BGshfh00TY25GxwaNfLX0kl5qDpaFyeMLz7_gLnG2nOuZf&s=wDCPAyTl5dUrWCTiZ3_4jY_4_LrO1j5CUEoZIWIF1jY&e= >.

NSF Writing Consultations

The consultations take place over three weeks, starting Monday, October 6

Essay draft submission deadline: Friday, September 26 (via this link
<https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdiD8fN25KFFs6sVvI00f0DJdVk1CLlHPJ8zD2_kKZVsHyqpg/viewform?usp=preview >
)

Through three weekly meetings with expert writing consultants, students
will receive individualized support in developing and writing their
application essays. In order to participate in these consultations, you
will need to submit drafts of your application essays. Once you have
submitted your drafts, you will be paired with a writing consultant who
will contact you to set up your meetings.

GRFP application deadlines are usually in mid to late October, but vary by
discipline. To be eligible for the NSF GRFP, an applicant must meet all of
the following eligibility criteria
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.nsf.gov_funding_opportunities_grfp-2Dnsf-2Dgraduate-2Dresearch-2Dfellowship-2Dprogram_nsf24-2D591_solicitation-23elig&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=57l3KMEuhT5lKBsgb6BGshfh00TY25GxwaNfLX0kl5qDpaFyeMLz7_gLnG2nOuZf&s=ogNOw7yCJgvVi-clGxA0BbxmFkXZhcCO9aqexqpSswg&e= >
by the application deadline. For additional information, see Application
Preparation and Submission Instructions
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__mx.technolutions.net_ss_c_u001.jxaZRujEt1lhE0WEp4QewBUvM6Y37KbNQiVlZO0BbnKSa1z2ViJ2239-2DUcbS1bp7w0CeDKRDR6OnKDqSwRGOhG-5FlZHps5orBP2OtaLhYt67bLy2N5yRWFnbg3Nprn5K0Qf71axu4Vv12oeDoot4KFrjjmyVuCTZ3gmMZKGg-2D-2Djk_4ir_q76-2DKDpVTEaR0gJCBrr2iQ_h2_h001.opdeY4-5FbN80gfsfeewN4PRncy4x14NlzqLix-5FgWxZ3A&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=T_qWyn748-1L3J6iCsnAvCLqb1OOQPXhQW53UE3dWJM&m=TSXpxIg6fZ3Mutw4nwqA7_rT1iWDFnH2-Its7V4v1uAcydfeWqGDc-11u7Vup63o&s=OE-_W7FzvC8McLUKrBZPspHtkWyh8b0ikJ3h04myfyM&e=>
and the GRFP Application video tutorial
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__mx.technolutions.net_ss_c_u001.ESQmFkfJY2HxaRefOslS73dLvhAnKFjuFrfZR3OhNnC6N7GVYXs0RJ2XoZZJZ22yJYALePUVbuq8ra-2DKvaxlgzsQsOcf7M-5FZvKyuMjMCfRUoSDSKdqs4Smoz9GbOrHhRUJlrJr9RQmtQPJP88cZEYQ_4ir_q76-2DKDpVTEaR0gJCBrr2iQ_h3_h001.sP1VBH2P5dRUL9qSrbOcPfY1BZ87tzIWK-2DnztZR6TYs&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=T_qWyn748-1L3J6iCsnAvCLqb1OOQPXhQW53UE3dWJM&m=TSXpxIg6fZ3Mutw4nwqA7_rT1iWDFnH2-Its7V4v1uAcydfeWqGDc-11u7Vup63o&s=i_ndkRq0TbSvPWIWybR3mLmwZg5kTo_lsLdk4karuHE&e=>
produced by the National Science Foundation.

Please reach out to gsas-finaid@columbia.edu
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__mx.technolutions.net_ss_c_u001.8XKdNyxJL2xmZaaY1Ol1lP3zHhjbGpgsvqPj-5FNdPLhjWUDIek5imJYEFE1m8OsMv_4ir_q76-2DKDpVTEaR0gJCBrr2iQ_h4_h001.Jis46XS69GIZU6DABr1NYDNyRaZst-2DvwwW8FNR30NdA&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=T_qWyn748-1L3J6iCsnAvCLqb1OOQPXhQW53UE3dWJM&m=TSXpxIg6fZ3Mutw4nwqA7_rT1iWDFnH2-Its7V4v1uAcydfeWqGDc-11u7Vup63o&s=e9UB77fdH22qZblX_Y3UqdN8ov7COW9UFFZTMrwcuXQ&e=>
with any questions.

Best Regards,
Helen

Helen Cen
Associate Director of Graduate Student Affairs

Engineering Student Affairs
Columbia University

*Have an emergency? *Contact Public Safety at (212) 854-5555

Concerned about a student, friend, or colleague?  Submit a Support Referral
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__cm.maxient.com_reportingform.php-3FColumbiaUniv-26layout-5Fid-3D1&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=57l3KMEuhT5lKBsgb6BGshfh00TY25GxwaNfLX0kl5qDpaFyeMLz7_gLnG2nOuZf&s=Sa2ek-MmQCxtrXdBmiUbaWxtEORWf7kyyTnzoa47YLg&e= >

*Connect with us!*
Academics  <seas-academics@columbia.edu> |  Wellness
<seas-wellness@columbia.edu> |  Community  <seas-life@columbia.edu> |
 Integrity  <https://studentconduct.columbia.edu/ > |  PDL  |
<https://pdl.engineering.columbia.edu/ > Career
<https://career.engineering.columbia.edu/ >

Confidentiality Notice: The information and any attachments contained in
this email are intended only for the personal and confidential use of the
recipient(s) named above. If the reader of this message is not the intended
recipient or an agent responsible for delivering it to the intended
recipient, you are hereby notified that you have received this in error and
that any review, disclosure, dissemination, distribution, or copying of
this message is strictly prohibited. If you have received this in error,
please notify me immediately by email, and delete the original message.

As of today no action has been taken.

Students need to confirm enrollment or request a waiver for the 2025-2026 plan year.

YOU MUST TAKE ACTION REGARDING HEALTH INSURANCE EVERY YEAR.

** What you need to do:
------------------------------------------------------------

OR

If you’ve already submitted your waiver request,
please allow 5-7 business days for processing.
You can check the status of your request on the Patient Portal.

** Optional:
------------------------------------------------------------
Enrollment in the voluntary Dependent, Dental or Vision plans must be completed each plan year. Enrollment does NOT automatically continue.

The deadline to enroll or submit a waiver is September 30, 2025

Questions?
Email the Student Health Insurance Office studentinsurance@columbia.edu (mailto:studentinsurance@columbia.edu) .

Be well,

Student Health Insurance Office

emails.subject: Advanced Standing Request Submitted on Your Behalf
emails.body:
Hi everyone,

I'm writing to let you know that I have submitted the advanced standing
request for your master's degree.

As a reminder about what this is
<https://www.cs.columbia.edu/education/phd/registration/ >:

*GSAS requires all PhD students to complete 60 total points of graduate
> coursework and 6 Residence Units for the degree. Upon completing the MS
> degree in SEAS (or at another institution prior to enrolling at Columbia),
> GSAS will award students with 30 points and 2 Residence Units of advanced
> standing toward the PhD. Therefore, students only need to complete 30
> additional points of graduate coursework and 4 Residence Units upon
> entering GSAS.*

This advanced standing request is flat-rate, so everyone gets 30 points.
The import process
<https://www.cs.columbia.edu/education/phd/requirements/breadth/imports/ >
is separate from this, and you must complete course imports in order to
satisfy Breadth requirements
<https://www.cs.columbia.edu/education/phd/requirements/breadth/ >. Because
you have already completed an MS, you are allowed to import up to all 6
electives. Distribution courses must be completed at Columbia either as a
course or comp exam.

Once the advanced standing request is approved, you will have 30 points,
and fall 2025 is your *3*rd out of 6 Residence Units needed to complete the
program. Please keep that in mind when pacing your 60 points. (You will
receive an email from SEAS when your request is approved.)

Best,
Da'Shante

*Da'Shante Smith*
Associate Director of Academic Administration and Student Services
Computer Science | Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science

She/Her/Hers - Why is this here?
<https://universitylife.columbia.edu/pronouns >

emails.subject: RE: Re: Advanced Standing Submission Confirmation | KS4190 SREEDHAR, KARTHIK
emails.body:
Greetings, 

    Thank you for your email. Your message has been received and our team will review as quickly as possible. We receive a high volume of emails and do our best to respond within 2-3 business days.

    If your email is regarding a registration adjustment (course add/drop, leave of absence, etc.), please be sure to discuss this with your academic department for assistance, if you have not done so already.

    We advise you to review our website <http://www.engineering.columbia.edu/academics/academic-resources/graduate-enrollment-services/change-status >, where you may find helpful information.

    Thank you,

    SEAS Academics Team
    Engineering Student Affairs

emails.subject: Re: Potential Student Membership Extension due to Back Injury
emails.body:
Thank you Karthik, If you're able to get a doctor's note stating you were
not able to work out the time that you missed, it would be helpful.

Please let me know and I will forward it to member services for you.

Thank you and Best regards,

Rick

[image: Blink Fitness]

[image: Sports Illustrated Best Budget Gym]

Rick Edwards (he/him)

*blink East Village | club manager*

o:212.202.3300

98 Avenue A

New York • NY 10038

blinkfitness.com <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.blinkfitness.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!UyKsQjZZa-_Zg13K4Fs2T4CpTWHBFZqVy1GepFMBWZ3mIOI5Srn0SNqwqs15t1le0GN7k95gLKueQyL50_uWxMnxN_DSDLQB$ >

On Thu, Sep 25, 2025 at 1:40 PM Karthik Sreedhar <ks4190@columbia.edu>
wrote:

> Hi,
>
> My name is Karthik, we spoke yesterday at the blink in east village
> regarding my back injury that prevented me from coming to the gym for about
> a month and a half in the middle of my 3 month student membership. I've
> attached my MRI results from August 5th that details the worsening of my
> L4-L5 disc, as well as the first message I had sent to my doctor regarding
> the pain on July 21st.
>
> I'm happy to come in-person to share the rest of the thread with my
> surgeon/his PA In person - I can also request an explicit note from my
> physical therapist/doctor if needed, but that may take a couple days for
> the turnaround.
>
> I sincerely appreciate your willingness to work with me - I completely
> understand if an extension in the membership is not possible, but I
> appreciate your understanding nonetheless!
>
> Thanks,
> Karthik
>

emails.snippet: Thank you Karthik, If you're able to get a doctor's note stating you were not able to work out the time that you missed, it would be helpful. Please let me know and I will forward it to member

emails.subject: RE: PID: 155638
emails.body:
Hi Karthik,

We will have a timeslot open at 12 pm on Monday. Could we reschedule for that time?

And yes, our RAs will be present in the Uris Hall BRL for the Cognitive Tasks study at 3.15 pm. The timeslot is still valid.

Thank you for understanding and we apologize again for the situation.

Kind regards,
—
Lucie Clifford
Behavioral Research Lab Manager
Office of the Vice Dean for Research
+1 212-853-8853
lc3654@gsb.columbia.edu<mailto:lc3654@gsb.columbia.edu>
[cid:image001.png@01DC2E22.64596C20]
From: Karthik Sreedhar <ks4190@columbia.edu>
Sent: Thursday, September 25, 2025 1:30 PM
To: Clifford, Lucie <lc3654@gsb.columbia.edu>
Cc: Brown, Derek <derekbrown@gsb.columbia.edu>; Sophie Cheng <sqc2103@barnard.edu>; crb2227@columbia.edu
Subject: Re: PID: 155638

Hi Lucie,

I cannot make it tomorrow unfortunately, but I can do Monday or Tuesday next week almost anytime after noon if either works.

I also am scheduled for a different study (Cognitive Tasks) at 3:15PM today - I am not sure if you know anything about that, but in case you do, is that time slot also invalid?

Thanks,
Karthik

On Thu, Sep 25, 2025 at 1:22 PM Clifford, Lucie <lc3654@gsb.columbia.edu<mailto:lc3654@gsb.columbia.edu>> wrote:
Hi Karthik,

We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience today. Unfortunately, the timeslots were posted incorrectly, which caused the confusion. Could we reschedule your session for a time tomorrow or early next week that works best for you? Please let us know your availability, and we will make sure everything is confirmed properly.

Thank you for your understanding, and again, we are truly sorry.

Kind regards,
—
Lucie Clifford
Behavioral Research Lab Manager
Office of the Vice Dean for Research
+1 212-853-8853
lc3654@gsb.columbia.edu<mailto:lc3654@gsb.columbia.edu>
[cid:image001.png@01DC2E22.64596C20]

Sent: Thursday, September 25, 2025 1:08 PM
To: Brown, Derek <derekbrown@gsb.columbia.edu<mailto:derekbrown@gsb.columbia.edu>>
Cc: Aria Simon <mbs2252@barnard.edu<mailto:mbs2252@barnard.edu>>; crb2227@columbia.edu<mailto:crb2227@columbia.edu>; Clifford, Lucie <lc3654@gsb.columbia.edu<mailto:lc3654@gsb.columbia.edu>>
Subject: Re: PID: 155638

Thank you! I’m leaving now, nobody is still here. Once again very sorry for the number of emails I sent in trying to get here.

On Sep 25, 2025, at 12:54 PM, Brown, Derek <derekbrown@gsb.columbia.edu<mailto:derekbrown@gsb.columbia.edu>> wrote:

Sorry about that! Forwarding your note to the Behavioral Lab team.
________________________________
From: Karthik Sreedhar <ks4190@columbia.edu<mailto:ks4190@columbia.edu>>
Sent: Thursday, September 25, 2025 12:52:52 PM
To: Aria Simon <mbs2252@barnard.edu<mailto:mbs2252@barnard.edu>>; crb2227@columbia.edu<mailto:crb2227@columbia.edu> <crb2227@columbia.edu<mailto:crb2227@columbia.edu>>; Brown, Derek <derekbrown@gsb.columbia.edu<mailto:derekbrown@gsb.columbia.edu>>
Subject: PID: 155638

Hi,

I was scheduled to do your behavioral lab study at 12:42 at B204 - I sent a lot of emails due to a station closure and me being worried I would be late, then also because I accidentally went to 204 in Uris instead of the Behavioral Lab. Very sorry about that!

But I got to B204 (picture attached) around 12:46 and nobody is here (not just in the room but in the entire hallway itself), I’ll wait another 10 or so minutes but please let me know if there was somewhere else I was meant to report!

Thanks,
Karthik

emails.subject: [ACTION REQUIRED] University Life Pre-Orientation Training
emails.body:
Dear Students,

This is a friendly reminder that you still need to complete the Pre-Orientation training provided by University Life.

At the moment, you may be seeing a pop-up notification about the incomplete training. Please be aware that failure to complete it will result in a full lockout of your Columbia University account as part of the sanctions on October 15th.

While some schools may have additional training for new students, all students, including those starting a new program are required to complete the University Life Pre-orientation training.

You can access the training here using your Columbia email: https://preorientation.universitylife.columbia.edu/  

If you have any questions please email us at preorientation@columbia.edu

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Kind regards,
Education & Training Team at University Life

emails.subject: DATE CHANGE: PhD Students Connect now Friday, 10/10
emails.body:
Dear Columbia Engineering doctoral students, candidates and Bridge
Scholars,

Please see details below for the first *PhD Students Connect* of the
academic year:

   - *Date: *Friday, October 10th,
   - *Time: *1-2 pm
   - *Location: *Mudd 524
   - *Registration: *No registration; first-come, first-served, but *CUID
   required *and offer only good until supplies last! :)
   - *Event Purpose: *For PhDs to connect over food

Although PhD Students Connect is usually on the first Friday of each month,
due to scheduling conflicts and room constraints, we had to pivot to next
Friday, October 10th. Please pardon any inconvenience.

Thank you, and look forward to seeing you all on Friday, 10/10, if not
sooner!

Cheers,
Andy

-- 

Andy Chae * He/Him/His *
Senior Director of Graduate Student Life
Engineering Student Affairs
Columbia Engineering

ac4897@columbia.edu  |  212-854-8930  |  Columbia Engineering

*Have an emergency? *Contact Public Safety at (212) 854-5555

*Connect with us!*
Academics <seas-academics@columbia.edu> |  Wellness
<seas-wellness@columbia.edu> |  Community <seas-life@columbia.edu> |

<https://career.engineering.columbia.edu/  >

emails.subject: RE: Re: FINAL WARNING BEFORE REGISTRATION HOLD: SEAS Mandatory Orientation Tutorials
emails.body:
Greetings, 

    Thank you for your email. Your message has been received and our team will review as quickly as possible. We receive a high volume of emails and do our best to respond within 2-3 business days.

    If your email is regarding a registration adjustment (course add/drop, leave of absence, etc.), please be sure to discuss this with your academic department for assistance, if you have not done so already.

    We advise you to review our website <http://www.engineering.columbia.edu/academics/academic-resources/graduate-enrollment-services/change-status >, where you may find helpful information.

    Thank you,

    SEAS Academics Team
    Engineering Student Affairs

Greetings from Amazon Mechanical Turk,

Congratulations, your Mechanical Turk account has been approved.  
Please follow this link to complete the registration process.  
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.mturk.com/mturk/beginsignin__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!VnVfGU4pIqCHWEhSk_UrMpIbKOWnHp3GQcqJFwvJjv2RznzEi00CfglKHP9Jgm2T1-kChnrWvk6ZlCFrOQRqcbUDmGtGdA$

Here's some important information about working on Mechanical Turk.

1) Requesters approve or reject your work within 30 days.
Requesters determine whether to pay you within 30 days of submitting 
your HITs.  Amazon Mechanical Turk does not determine when to approve 
or reject and does not estimate when your HITs will be approved.  In 
the event you have questions about the content of a HIT or the approval 
status you can contact the Requester directly: 
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.mturk.com/mturk/help?helpPage=worker*work_rejected__;Iw!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!VnVfGU4pIqCHWEhSk_UrMpIbKOWnHp3GQcqJFwvJjv2RznzEi00CfglKHP9Jgm2T1-kChnrWvk6ZlCFrOQRqcbWdIwBj-g$ 

2) Your work quality is important.
Completing HITs accurately will ensure that Requesters will want you to 
continue to work for them.  Read instructions thoroughly, answer HITs 
accurately, and return HITs you are unable to complete correctly, to 
build a positive reputation as a Worker.  Keep in mind that returned or 
abandoned HITs will not affect your rating.

3) Report HITs that violate the Mechanical Turk policies:
You should not provide personal information in the HIT or register for 
another website while working HITs on Mechanical Turk.  Please report 
any violating HITs using the "violates Amazon Mechanical Turk policies" 
link at the bottom right of the HIT.  To see examples of HITs that 
violate the policies, visit this link: 
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.mturk.com/mturk/help?helpPage=policies*violation_examples__;Iw!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!VnVfGU4pIqCHWEhSk_UrMpIbKOWnHp3GQcqJFwvJjv2RznzEi00CfglKHP9Jgm2T1-kChnrWvk6ZlCFrOQRqcbXfs_YEoQ$

4) Keep your computer secure:
When you complete HITs on Mechanical Turk, you are accessing the 
internet to perform work for Requesters that are not Amazon.  We 
recommend that you secure your computer with the latest operating 
system security updates and virus protection software, update your 
browser and plug-ins with the latest versions, and use caution when 
directed to other websites or asked to download software.

5) Payment delays and daily HIT limit for new Workers:
For quality assurance purposes, new Workers can only complete a limited 
number of HITs (Human Intelligent Tasks) each day.  Payments from 
Requesters are delayed for 10 days until you have submitted at least 
one HIT a day for at least 10 days.  Your payments will show the 
"Approved Pending Payment" status in your dashboard during this delay 
period.

6) Amazon Payments does not charge a periodic fee, ATM withdrawal fee,
cash reload fee, ATM balance inquiry fee, customer service fee,
inactivity fee, dormancy fee, disbursement fee or any other sort of
fee for use of its Amazon Payments account, which is necessary at this
time for US Workers to disburse their earnings. Amazon Payments
accounts do not have an overdraft or credit feature and are not FDIC
insured.

Additional information can be found within our FAQ, 
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.mturk.com/mturk/help?helpPage=worker__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!VnVfGU4pIqCHWEhSk_UrMpIbKOWnHp3GQcqJFwvJjv2RznzEi00CfglKHP9Jgm2T1-kChnrWvk6ZlCFrOQRqcbXVkyCopQ$
We're excited to have you join the Amazon Mechanical Turk Worker 
Community!

Sincerely,
Amazon Mechanical Turk
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.mturk.com__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!VnVfGU4pIqCHWEhSk_UrMpIbKOWnHp3GQcqJFwvJjv2RznzEi00CfglKHP9Jgm2T1-kChnrWvk6ZlCFrOQRqcbVHhrFkRQ$

emails.subject: Supporting Our International Student Community
emails.body:
October 8, 2025
Dear members of the Columbia community,

With the fall semester well underway and midterms approaching, I want to take a moment to reflect on an integral part of our community at Columbia: our international students and scholars. They strengthen the social and intellectual fabric of our ecosystem, and we are deeply committed to supporting their success.

I am so grateful for the immense efforts that were undertaken across our institution this past spring and summer to support our incoming international students. Nearly all of the international students and scholars who chose Columbia have made their way to New York and are now here with us on campus.

This is no small accomplishment, and it is one worth celebrating. It reflects the determination of our international students, faculty, and researchers and their families, as well as the unwavering commitment of our staff and administrators.

Some of the work undertaken:

	- 
Schools and programs across Columbia began their work early to ensure students had ample time to apply for visas. The International Students and Scholars Office (ISSO) issued I-20s with a 24-hour turnaround time, partnering with all Columbia schools to support incoming and continuing international students as circumstances evolved.
	- 
International student advisors provided close support to I-20 holders throughout their visa application process, offering individualized advising, troubleshooting, and tracking student experiences around the globe to better tailor guidance to different geographies.
	- 
Forecasting possible arrival delays, the University explored alternate academic paths to create flexibility within the 2025-2026 academic calendar, preparing potential routes to ensure that any international students arriving to campus late could quickly adapt to their coursework.And our commitment to our international students and scholars hasn’t stopped with their arrival:
	- 
We continue to keep the campus community apprised with pertinent updates and guidance around changing U.S. immigration policy. Since the start of the academic year alone, guidance has been issued on the 
https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=dd86822e66cc754ec121775da692f414e43d25165b4a15e4f03128b82d1b36366ef454aa9912afb0562241e08f67d61569cd0a58a8879f1c4eb09d83c0449111  
proclamation restricting H-1B workers ; the U.S. Department of State announcement on 
https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=dd86822e66cc754ed20124fb98328575fbae6781463e37c627611d76f57362adb1cec5838721aab4ce9260cb40f20b0a5d2dadd634c64e00927f844f517a2e66  
adjudicating visa appointments ; and the Department of Homeland Security’s proposed rule on 
https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=dd86822e66cc754e93dfd202c1421e0b625f8a111fc146529c2a8efe6067668a4bee38ac1fe97c93b2a6524e7628d232b45a79dbe401e0d6eed6fb8e568f9bce  
Duration of Status . These updates, and more, are available at: 
https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=dd86822e66cc754e466a2c57179e342f8fac2a86a23ca62f2b4fd1a3fa6a5526b5145b59a2797b6028b20c2bb9ff91583088eeed497646df151d1c135d78129d  
ISSO news page  and the 
https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=dd86822e66cc754e4c4511893e021ee3c0a51a1085694a23d50e4a47d0c34dabd0282cc5b2e3e0060092e1d7974ffeea599bcb5524e8c30884ac39632f5f2f53  
Immigration Resources page .
	- 
Over 1,000 new students joined the New International Student Welcome Gathering in Lerner Hall this September, and over 1,800 incoming students attended pre-arrival programs on topics such as housing, immigration guidance, and academic success. Stay up-to-date on upcoming international community programming by exploring ISSO’s 
https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=dd86822e66cc754ee28609fd632344dc7943776f952b44f181a61cd6362ae82e076151fa58f1782a8989b87dd07a48798fd568988064ba9a0d6a8b4c34cf1cdb  
programs and events .
	- 
We are also pleased to announce our renewed commitment to the International Student Hardship Fund. Since April, the University has disbursed nearly $500,000 to international students facing unanticipated costs due to travel or visa issues. For more information on eligibility and the process to apply for support, please visit the 
https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=dd86822e66cc754e872ce884d531e5f3837d1c32ca36a0d0ca1d8a52557b9c636fe3ad8dbf7b261abd98940d1c8e2875c86e93e8a904c953eaec933d06801505  
Fund’s website .

More resources:

	- Immigration Webinars: On Monday, October 6, from 3:00 pm to 4:30 pm, Columbia hosted another webinar with immigration attorney Dan Berger. Since December 2024, this webinar series has brought together nearly 5,000 community members and answered over 1,600 community questions on how to navigate new immigration policies. Columbia will continue to host these informational webinars for students, faculty, researchers, and staff throughout the academic year.
	- Contact ISSO: ISSO’s information phone lines are staffed seven days a week, on weekdays from 8:00 am to 7:00 pm and on weekends from 9:00 am to 3:00 pm. Weekday appointments are also available 
https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=dd86822e66cc754e39d369117f3c35b82a2991288305425594280dc253ad8020cf3ea645d97ad5e9e0ed239c205f510d35f3282411f5140af38b6671a11b90b6  
for booking  to speak with a student advisor, on Zoom or in-person. Same-day appointments for urgent issues are available. You can learn 
https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=dd86822e66cc754e31cc117b19e6209a7a11d788db9412c018ef1890b311ead42920716ddb8972c05121b66510601ffe4617731832f455609c3e873cd40f8db0  
how to contact an ISSO  advisor on the 
https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=dd86822e66cc754e597b253f9490c76b3dcaaa65f2ad72e28e3d1356a7fffbf7eb7c26d62c02baf412899aa6a6bd3ecc30b310488994b15e2c4297ad96b129e8  
ISSO website .We understand that this moment feels tenuous, often fraught, for our international students and scholars. We are committed to providing all of the resources we can and to creating an atmosphere in which they can thrive as essential members of our community. As I have noted before, we support the same rights to free expression, due process, and fair treatment for all of our students. The world comes to New York, and to Columbia, and we are the better for it. I am grateful to everyone who stepped forward to welcome and support our international community this year.

Sincerely,

Claire Shipman
Acting President, Columbia University in the City of New York

Columbia University

 116th and Broadway

 New York, NY 10027

emails.subject: FINAL WARNING BEFORE REGISTRATION HOLD: SEAS Mandatory Orientation Tutorials
emails.body:
Dear student,

We are reaching out with a final warning with regard to the SEAS
Orientation Tutorials requirement, which is mandatory for all SEAS graduate
students.

At this time, our records indicate that you have either not begun the
tutorials or have not yet successfully completed all of them.

We are kindly asking that you complete this SEAS requirement by Monday,
October 13, 2025.

If you do not complete the Orientation Tutorials by this date, you will
receive a registration hold that will prevent you from registering for
future courses. Once placed, the hold will not be lifted until the
tutorials have been completed.

The Orientation Tutorials are available on Courseworks via the following
course link: https://courseworks2.columbia.edu/courses/161622 . Please note
that this requirement is in addition to the pre-orientation tutorials
<https://universitylife.columbia.edu/welcome-columbia-pre-orientation-tutorials >
and the university-wide Community Citizenship Initiative
<https://universitylife.columbia.edu/CCI > (CCI) mandated by the Office of
University Life.

As previously noted, you must receive a perfect score (100%) on each
tutorial/quiz in order for this requirement to be considered complete. You
may re-take quizzes as needed until you achieve a perfect score. Kindly
note that the Food Pantry quiz is optional but strongly recommended.

Let us know of any questions or concerns that you might have.

Sincerely,

SEAS Academics

Engineering Student Affairs

emails.subject: Final key step before joining Prolific as an expert
emails.body:
Hello Karthik,

At Prolific, we are committed to maintaining a high standard of quality and trust across our platform. To maintain the integrity of our platform and ensure a reliable space for both participants and researchers, we need to take steps to verify the background of certain individuals who may be considered ‘experts’ in a particular area, before onboarding.

As part of this process, we’re requesting that such participants and prospective participants provide us with the following:

* A link to their LinkedIn profile

This will help us to verify that the information shared is correct in order to prevent fraud through the provision of false or misleading information.You can add the information requested by clicking on the button below, and read more about how we handle your data in our Participant Privacy Notice ( https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.notion.so/Participant-Privacy-Notice-6b6cb4f60a674d96a1433bff6412215b?pvs=21__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!SI306Os0NjDd4RBC3VJa9L2IU4TPR8M09DWbInWFe6jIIQSdx4WWREn8VRfxvIOCZKA6-yHyJCPj36my27EYrHwJkI0UNQ$  ).

Tell us more about your experience ( https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://app.prolific.com/register/participant/waitlist/?campaign_code=C15394Q3&waitlist_id=68e5440e1b46ef76e39c6345__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!SI306Os0NjDd4RBC3VJa9L2IU4TPR8M09DWbInWFe6jIIQSdx4WWREn8VRfxvIOCZKA6-yHyJCPj36my27EYrHzO0HJOdA$  )Thanks,

The Prolific Team

Information you provide in response to our waitlist survey is used to enable us to identify individuals with certain skills or types of background for Prolific, and is processed in accordance with our Participant Privacy Notice ( https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://participant-marketing.prolific.com/e/c/eyJlIjoxNjc0NjgsImVtYWlsX2lkIjoiZXhhbXBsZSIsImhyZWYiOiJodHRwczovL3Byb2xpZmljLm5vdGlvbi5zaXRlL1BhcnRpY2lwYW50LVByaXZhY3ktTm90aWNlLTZiNmNiNGY2MGE2NzRkOTZhMTQzM2JmZjY0MTIyMTViIiwidCI6MTc0NzMwNjY4MX0/9000dd326fabe28edfd275b123ac4825a2b92403bda86eee800a0af22cb2af5a__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!SI306Os0NjDd4RBC3VJa9L2IU4TPR8M09DWbInWFe6jIIQSdx4WWREn8VRfxvIOCZKA6-yHyJCPj36my27EYrHzu50H3AQ$  ).
