emails.subject: Happy October! 🍂 | EGSC Weekly Newsletter
emails.body:
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/  >
*Hey Grad SEAS!*
Happy first week of October! We’ve officially wrapped up the first month of
the Fall 2025 semester!

This week, we are excited to host *Entrepreneurship Mixer & Pitch Night* on
*Wednesday*! Applications for the *Professional Development Scholarship* close
on *Saturday*!

Don't forget to *follow us on Instagram @columbiaegsc
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.instagram.com/columbiaegsc/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjRTdLJKk$ > *to stay updated on all our
events!
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/department-representatives  >
*Welcome your Fall 2025 Department Representatives!* These student leaders
are here to *advocate *for your needs, *cultivate community* within each
department, and *make sure your voices are heard* across Graduate SEAS.
They're your link to all things EGSC!
Meet Your DRs Here!
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/department-representatives  >

Are you a current Columbia University graduate student founder eager to
pitch your business idea? Or are you looking to connect with other founders
and like-minded peers? If so, join us for an inspiring evening of pitches,
networking, and conversation!

Co-sponsored by the *Lang Center at Columbia Business School *and* the
Engineering Graduate Student Council*, this event offers a unique
opportunity to share ideas, meet fellow entrepreneurs, and build meaningful
connections across Columbia’s schools.

How It Works:

*When you register, you’ll answer a few quick questions—including whether
you’d like to pitch. *If selected, you’ll have the chance to showcase your
idea and meet potential collaborators. After the pitches, we’ll transition
into a relaxed mixer where you can continue networking.

This is a *casual and welcoming* environment—just bring yourself and your
enthusiasm! Whether you're actively working on a venture or simply curious,
we’d love to have you join us.

We look forward to seeing you there!

*THE ENTREPRENEURSHIP MIXER & PITCH NIGHT IS SOLD OUT!*
Join the Waitlist Here! <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/jsoog0en__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjYZrkpFE$ >
Join us at our *weekly GBMs*, whether you are a Department Representative
or not! We meet on Fridays from 4-5 pm in Mudd 524.
In keeping with the highest ideals of the EGSC, the *Professional
Development Scholarship Program* is awarded to outstanding graduate
students in the Columbia University Fu Foundation School of Engineering and
Applied Science (SEAS) as a means of defraying costs associated with their
professional endeavors.  Those chosen as EGSC Professional Development
Scholars are expected to make a broader impact on the larger Columbia
Engineering community by sharing their knowledge and experience.

Past recipients have used the Scholarship to fund *memberships* *to
professional societies, certification exam fees, and the costs of attending
academic or professional conferences*.

*AWARD AMOUNT:*
Applicants may request *up to $1,000* per application. The final award
amount and the number of awards per semester will be decided by the
evaluating committee. Any unused funds are non-transferable and may not be
used for expenses unrelated to those explicitly stated in the funded
application.

Please note that original receipts and proof of acceptance/attendance (if
applicable) are required in order to be eligible for reimbursement.
Furthermore, for travel expenses, stays exceeding 1 day before and after
the event must be justified and will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

*FALL APPLICATION DEADLINE:*
*Saturday, October 11th, 2025 at 5:00PM EST.*
Apply Here!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScukPIxcTNSLHAOkoMMPw8z1UPCiQgjb6sLuwFhgsV7JEPqDQ/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjDtVyqLU$ >
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://exponentrecruit.azurewebsites.net/Register/302__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHj4r9KK3U$ >

🎓 *Calling all Columbia PhD Students! *🎓
Are you curious about how your research expertise can shape real-world
solutions beyond academia?
Join us for an exclusive PhD-focused career seminar with Exponent, a global
leader in engineering and scientific consulting! 🌎💡

🔷 *When:* Monday, October 13, 2025
🔷 *Time:* 12:00 – 1:00 PM
🔷 *Where:* Hamilton 603
🔷* Who: *PhD Students only
🔷 Lunch will be provided!

Meet Exponent’s team of PhD-trained professionals as they share how they
apply their technical backgrounds to solve some of the world’s most complex
challenges — from thermal sciences and biomedical engineering to health
sciences.

💼 This is a fantastic opportunity to explore consulting career pathways,
connect directly with Exponent’s team, and learn about job
opportunities tailored for PhDs.

👉 *RSVP here <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://exponentrecruit.azurewebsites.net/Register/302__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHj4r9KK3U$ >*
📃 *See flyer for details*

*Hosted by the Engineering Graduate Student Council (EGSC)*
*Carleton Commons Social October Edition* is happening *next Friday*! Stop
by to grab some snacks, pizza, and drinks, and socialize with fellow SEAS
graduates!

This event is free and only for *current graduate engineering students*!
Registration is required and will take place at the door! *CUID with EP
sticker and government ID required!*

*Changes to Intercampus Shuttle Evening Schedule Beginning October 13*
To increase access between Columbia University Irving Medical Center
(CUIMC) and Morningside Campus in the evening hours, Columbia
Transportation has analyzed ridership data and will implement schedule
changes on *Monday, October 13*.
Join the Graduate Earth & Environmental Engineering Network (GrEEEN) on *Friday
October 10th from 9AM-11AM* for a Morningside Park Cleanup & Gardening
Event! This is a great opportunity to get outside and give back to our
community! RSVP is required
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdQojDSNGaCCD7cQ-c8s7VprYC9OvQznjx_1Zei9mFV8Yk0lA/viewform?usp=sharing&ouid=107239468865426412414__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Uj7xo1oEOft6OLt0FJZ8rEjRaIqCFJnqzyUmzMqPiPO8QxpKgbsqlpCzWZ32pqp9HmYmIeQnPR7aKlTu6ZRhlw6bPHBLnN58EfEeHlI$>
Interested in joining the *Coalition on Race and Ethnicity in Engineering
(CORE^2)* Eboard? Apply to be an executive member! This is a great
opportunity to utilize/gain leadership skills while simultaneously
enriching the way CORE^2 serves our members.

Join us in our mission to foster a community that provides academic,
social, and professional support for traditionally underrepresented
students in graduate engineering. We hope you are excited to fortify the
POC community in and around our university in these ways with us.
Apply Today!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSewY0za5v5I_UQXX_n_Ypt_D537mwAup-z4ZWgLm2KBdjP6SA/viewform?usp=dialog__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!TruXzRtu85xKuJPIemjZDvW0sxaZqv-xt6ZMR2O_VeI4vSVRNUjkjGOcdDHciOnzbbysr4hOKJ2Bi6rvsJ55vXcA$>
Interested in learning about and discussing topics at the intersection of
science, society, and the environment? Join the biweekly Novel Engineers
book club
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://join.slack.com/t/novelengineer-caw9502/shared_invite/zt-3dkk3vyg2-hh3UBWmTX78uOR0tuEDgEA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QT3_O1cskr99jWtniGYmKFPkqPNe4Sfl8vlcie4YRYG6DlqMFvrUXXscZhcFPaUaxyfb--FxBxzHsZd3vVSYaoUvhmGiIk0miIJZrpmi$>,
sponsored by GrEEEN! This semester we are reading *It’s Not That Radical:
Climate Action to Transform Our World* by Mikaela Loach. Our first meeting
is *Thursday, Oct 9, 5:30-6:30pm* in Mudd 1045! Books and food provided
first-come, first-served!
Interested in joining *Columbia’s only student club dedicated to
Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI)*? At *Neurotech X Columbia*, you’ll find
opportunities to engage in cutting-edge research projects  and connect with
peers passionate about neurotechnology. We welcome all members of the
Columbia community to join us — please register through this Google Form:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/1r6jLNyCo6pbxXR3jEqPT0a_FzcpqlB4d4iCH3_75ZbQ__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHj_bk3r6Q$ 
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/1r6jLNyCo6pbxXR3jEqPT0a_FzcpqlB4d4iCH3_75ZbQ__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!U66AXcO3CK09cjy5CEinktcs0lyktt1ow3JxvVucDXBZcW_LeYTqdaFHhq0nJeCzhWoF8A1KI_e8L7oaewiVfhUko8lKWg$>
STEM Starters accepts both Columbia post-doc and graduate student
volunteers for our free science events for kids! All volunteers must go
through Protection of Minors training (~1hr) for the safety of our
attendees.

*Please record your email here
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSe6d2t9FB4bdFJtgd3aD7DeLob6h8ZbKZmVrGWJF4HjS0uQMA/viewform?pli=1__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjOx4QMPY$ >
and
we'll be in touch about next steps! *

For more information, visit our website at:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.womeninscienceatcolumbia.org/stemstarters__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHja0SxFgk$  or contact us by
email: STEMStartersCU@gmail.com.

*** No-cost flu vaccines at these events are available to ALL ELIGIBLE
STUDENTS* <https://www.health.columbia.edu/flu#text-18266  > regardless of
credit load or health insurance plan.No appointment needed: just bring your
CUID.***

*Wednesday, October 8: 3rd Fl. Board Rooms, Geffen Hall (Manhattanville)*
9:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m.

Please note that you are not able to join the line prior to opening or
after closing.

For those over 65 years of age
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.cdc.gov/flu/highrisk/65over.htm__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X7f859X_d379DEJVUzq_6MK7S-OiquHc7Pmxyfto6wyujDDIv8qCILkURv8l3bSzvpIpCPWNTPJ17JFIMV1EVglHHG9ALunTdXs$>,
Columbia Health has a limited supply of the high-dose vaccine. Please speak
to the nurse administering your vaccine if you are eligible for the
high-dose flu vaccine.

***Early bird exclusive****
Each day, the first 150 community members who get their flu shot at the
event will receive an exclusive “Fight Flu” hot/cold pack!
RSVP Here!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeJDkw_2wSSNtV-ajNrS97xSEPgzuBhy2XvVXUlVSJS1dpnGg/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjb9Gi_cA$ >
Looking for a hands-on, impactful internship next summer?YC just released
its Summer 2026 internship list, featuring roles at top AI startups where
you’ll work directly with founders, tackle meaningful projects, and gain
first-hand experience building the future of AI. Many past interns have
even gone on to launch their own startups and join YC as founders! Check
out the opportunities below!
YC Summer 2026 Internship List <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://znsrc.com/c/kfmvencctd__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjWPtS2IE$ >
*We are thrilled to announce that applications for Nucleate's **Flagship
Activator Program* <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.org/activator/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjugSYS_o$ >* are **NOW OPEN!*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjW9FYRJo$ >
Are you an academic trainee (Post-doc/PhD/MBA/MD/JD/MS) eager to build a
life science startup? Whether your passion lies in *therapeutics,
diagnostics, medtech, synthetic biology*, or fields like *food, energy,
agriculture, or consumer products*, this is your opportunity to transform
your research into a groundbreaking venture!
What We Offer:
●      *Completely Free:* Nucleate never takes equity or charges fees for
its programs
●      *Mentorship from Industry Leaders:* Receive guidance from
experienced mentors.
●      *Comprehensive Educational Programming:* Learn from the best in
workshops and seminars.
●      *Global Community: *Join a network of peers, alumni, and leaders
across 18+ cities worldwide.
●      *Investor Introductions:* Get connected with investors ready to
support your breakthrough.
●      *No Forced Incorporation:* Focus on your venture at your own pace.
If you have an idea (i.e., you’re an Inventor), you can apply for either
our *Bio* or *Eco* track:
●      *Bio Track*: For innovators in therapeutics, diagnostics, or tools.
●      *Eco Track*: Focused on food & agriculture, chemicals & energy, or
consumer products.
*Don’t have an idea yet?* No worries! You can still join a team as a
*scientific,
business, or clinical contributor* through our matching process.
*Got a team already?* Your collaborators can come on board as *team
affiliates*, so you all get the chance to learn and build together.
*Applications* <https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjW9FYRJo$ >* are reviewed on a rolling
basis through October 20. Early submission is encouraged for early team
matching*.
*Check out our **FAQs page*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://drive.google.com/file/d/1WV3LQJYHbdauBCrYzPAjDUarJPJGrK5a/view__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHj9jjroOY$ >* if
you have any questions*
To learn more, join us at one of the following info sessions. Please
register to attend, food and drink will be served!

*Sign up for a virtual info session or Live Q&A session with Activator
Alumni and Mentors*:

   1. Nucleate Activator - Insider Session:
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/pcjqooak?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjpO_9sno$ > Tuesday, October
   7, at 6 PM

*What about IP?** Nucleate Activator is an education-focused, non-dilutive
program. We do not take any equity or IP, and all programming is designed
to be non-confidential. Inventors applying to the program must secure
approval from their PI and TTO before final acceptance.*
*About Nucleate*
Nucleate <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjMCrbM0g$ > is a student-run nonprofit organization
that facilitates the formation of new life science ventures. Our
equity-free Activator program <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/activator/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjKJjhgeQ$ > supports
emerging bioentrepreneurs coming from academia who plan to tackle the
biggest challenges in human health (Bio Track) and sustainability (Eco
Track) by building life science companies. Since our inception seven years
ago, we have guided 600+ scientific teams through their earliest stages,
leading to the formation of more than 180 companies that have collectively
raised over $500M. Our alumni are commercializing breakthrough technologies
such as protein sequencing (Glyphic <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://glyphic.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjF6MlXiE$ >), AI drug design
(Manifold Bio <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.manifold.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjsmPmjDU$ >, Concerto
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.concertobio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHj2y45vfU$ >), biomanufacturing (Foray Biosciences
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://foraybio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjx638V6c$ >) and many more.
*Apply*
Apply here <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjY9iGYdw$ > before *October 20th, 2025*.
*To Learn more about Nucleate visit our **Linktree*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://linktr.ee/nucleatehq__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjoc_N9r4$ >
[image: Instagram] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://instagram.com/columbiaegsc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjjGyYd-I$ >
[image: LinkedIn] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/company/egsccolumbia__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X6ylyYAthG63LvPutNW3jBxxEohDxpLUAKy8VENcPfYxlczkxBdQdNcSXect3JyhR0l6OfehBM1hIiHikgrp9pf79AzLioDOPu0YDguhehHjOZAr8uw$ >

We're making some changes to our PayPal legal agreements

We're making some changes to our PayPal legal agreements.

Hello, Karthik Sreedhar

We're making some changes to our legal agreements that will apply to you

There is no action needed from you today, but if you would like to learn more, you can find details about these changes, when they apply and what you can do if you want to decline the changes on our policy update page. You can also view these changes by visiting PayPal.com, clicking 'Legal' at the bottom of the page and then selecting 'Policy Updates'.

If you have questions about any of these changes or your account, please don't hesitate to get in touch with us.

"Your Privacy Matters: Understanding Your Privacy Rights"

We are required to provide you with a copy of the US Consumer Privacy Notice. If you live in California, North Dakota, or Vermont, please also see the Supplemental Notice. They will be effective November 17, 2025.

We have made updates to our affiliate and non-affiliate sharing. Please take a moment to read how PayPal collects, uses and discloses your personal information and what your rights are with respect to that data, such as how you may limit our sharing with certain affiliated and non-affiliated third parties for marketing purposes.

You may also review the full PayPal Privacy Statement, which will be in effect November 17, 2025. You can submit your privacy preferences at any time by accessing your data and privacy settings.

Thank you for being a PayPal customer.
Help & Contact | Security | Apps

PayPal is committed to preventing fraudulent emails. Emails from PayPal will always contain your full name. Learn to identify phishing

Please don't reply to this email. To get in touch with us, click Help & Contact.

is
seeking its next cohort of brilliant minds ready to invent cutting-edge
technologies! Research internships for Summer of 2026 are now open to
applicants pursuing PhD and Master’s degrees in the following research
areas: Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning, AR, VR & 360
Photography, Audio, Computer Vision, Imaging & Video, Content Intelligence,
Data Intelligence, Document Intelligence, Graphics (2D & 3D), Human
Computer Interaction, Intelligent Agents & Assistants, Natural Language
Processing, and Systems & Languages. Our 2026 Summer internships will be
offered as hybrid roles, co-located with Adobe Research mentors.

Interns collaborate directly with one or more Adobe researchers on our
staff as well as with Adobe’s world-class product and design teams. Work by
Adobe researchers and interns is frequently presented at top-tier academic
conferences and may be integrated into Adobe’s products, reaching millions
of users worldwide. Adobe Research is especially interested in fostering
ongoing relationships that last beyond the internship and that may even
contribute to an intern’s PhD thesis.
Apply Now

emails.subject: Compensation and Stipend Increases
emails.body:
https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=795c43b86634d02c6c83f290f00711e1f0347cd21cbc4bfb60f38005396d89a71764c46735b99e9b8533ad645c5274cd3f21d6a07db1ef1bfb7e0a27bc032348  

October 6, 2025

Dear Students and Faculty,

As you are aware, the University and the Student Workers of Columbia union (SWC) have made little progress in negotiating a successor contract since our first and only bargaining session in March. Information on 
https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=795c43b86634d02cae31222a4670402928f4e0f2ba63f10e76ae52acb4a00e4279abf1b36bf8431c8c0759e531ad9c2cdab02061da5ada790a53892b99ff9b2e  
our most recent bargaining attempt  is available. Still, the University remains fully committed to supporting our graduate student community and student employees.

Columbia has some of the finest young scholars in the world, who are focused on their coursework, teaching, and research, and who contribute much to making Columbia the great institution that it is. Accordingly, although the University continues to navigate difficult financial headwinds that will persist for the foreseeable future, Columbia will implement the following increases to student support, applied pro rata effective October 16, 2025:

	- Compensation will increase by 3% for PhD student employees who hold a teaching or research appointment in a guaranteed year of support in GSAS, SEAS, the VP&S Coordinated Doctoral Programs in Biomedical Sciences, the School of Nursing, the Columbia Business School, the Mailman School of Public Health, GSAPP, Journalism, SIPA, and the School of Social Work. This increase will apply to the pay period beginning October 16, which is paid on November 7. PhD students receiving stipends at an on-appointment rate, including those beyond their guaranteed years of funding who have been released from teaching duties, will also receive 3% increases. Stipends payments will be issued the week of October 27, reflecting the increase for fall, prorated from the October 16 effective date.

	- 
PhD students not on appointment in guaranteed years of funding will also receive 3% increases to their minimum support stipends. Stipends payments will be issued the week of October 27, reflecting the increase for fall, prorated from the October 16 effective date.

	- 
In each academic unit, compensation will increase by 3% for undergraduate students, Master’s students, non-PhD doctoral students, and PhD students beyond their guaranteed years of support who hold appointments as Teaching Assistants, Teaching Fellows, Preceptors, Readers, Teaching Assistants III, Graduate Research Assistants and Department Research Assistants. This increase will apply to the pay period beginning October 16, which is paid on November 7.

	- 
The University will also increase the minimum hourly rate for student employees who perform instructional or research work from $22.50/hour to $23.00/hour, starting October 20, 2025.

With the academic year now well underway, the University is disappointed that the union has not returned to the bargaining table or responded to the University’s proposals. Most recently, after canceling an agreed-upon bargaining session on September 11, without explanation, SWC refused to bargain at a scheduled meeting today, October 6. The union has posted that the University “canceled” today’s session. The University’s bargaining team was present at the neutral Interchurch location from the agreed-upon start time and remained ready to bargain. SWC’s delegation arrived an hour late to present demands but would not stay to negotiate or discuss scheduling future sessions, asking instead for email replies. The full update on the scheduled 
https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=795c43b86634d02cae31222a4670402928f4e0f2ba63f10e76ae52acb4a00e4279abf1b36bf8431c8c0759e531ad9c2cdab02061da5ada790a53892b99ff9b2e  
October 6 bargaining session  is available.

Additional information regarding negotiations is available under the 
https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=795c43b86634d02cc3a3f1caf024db31540ee050187aa84f36f67631bbe80e910ad1158c2283fbe06c167d419e6f587632755178dbb7e70f15cb8168116e09b3  
Current Negotiations  tab on the Student Benefits website.

Thank you for everything you do to support our community and advance Columbia’s teaching and research mission.

Sincerely,

Amy Hungerford

Dean and Executive Vice President, Faculty of Arts and Sciences

Cas Holloway

Special Advisor to Acting President Claire Shipman

Copyright (c) 2025 Columbia University. All rights reserved.You are receiving this email because you are part of the Columbia University community.

Columbia University  - Office of Public Affairs

 402 Low Library, MC 4321, 535 W. 116th St

 New York, NY 10027

emails.subject: URGENT: Missing Final Official Transcripts
emails.body:
You will receive a hold.

Office of Graduate Admissions | Columbia Engineering

                        Office of Graduate Admissions                      

Dear Karthik,

                      You are receiving this email because it appears that we have not received all of your final official transcripts and/or proof of degree conferral.

                      🔹 Deadline
                                                                     All final official transcripts must be received by Wednesday, October 15, 2025.                      

                      🔹 How to Submit
                                                                     Transcripts must be certified and securely sent directly to Columbia University, Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science (or finaldocs@columbia.edu) from your institution's Registrar's Office.

                        Students who have attended a college or university in China will be required to contact CHESICC to arrange submission of a "Verification Report of China Higher Education Student's Academic Transcript" AND a "Verification Report of China Higher Education Qualification Certificate" (for those earning a degree in China) to Columbia at finaldocs@columbia.edu.

                        Students who have attended a university that is not in China or the United States must submit an electronic transcript if your school offers secure online delivery to finaldocs@columbia.edu. If the university cannot send transcripts electronically via a secure, password-protected system, applicants must order a Course-by-Course Evaluation and International Credential Advantage Package to be sent electronically to finaldocs@columbia.edu from World Education Services (WES).

                      🔹 Missing Documents
                        Official Transcript for University Of California, Berkeley
Official Transcript for University of California: Berkeley
Official Transcript for University of California: Berkeley
                                               
                      🔹 Important
                                                                     If your transcripts are not received by the deadline, a registration hold will be placed on your account for the Spring semester.

                        Please log in to your Status Portal  <https://apply.engineering.columbia.edu/manage/ >to view your Enrollment Checklist and confirm what items remain outstanding.                      

                        🔹 Tips
                                               
                        Students who have attended US institutions: Transcripts sent to Columbia University, Undergraduate Admissions office cannot be retrieved.  Ensure you are selecting Columbia University, Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science

                        If official test scores are missing:  GRE/TOEFL scores must be sent to school code 2111                      

                      If you have questions about how to submit final official transcripts, please refer to our FAQ <https://www.gradengineering.columbia.edu/faq/admitted-students >. If you believe you have received this email in error, please email finaldocs@columbia.edu. 

                      Thank you,
                       
                      Office of Graduate Admissions
                      The Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science
                      Columbia University
seasgradmit@columbia.edu

COLUMBIA UNIVERSITY
                      The Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science
                      500 W. 120th Street #1220
                      New York, NY 10027 
                      Tel (212) 854-4688
https://www.gradengineering.columbia.edu/admissions

emails.subject: PhD Rep Nomination
emails.body:
Hi Karthik,

You've been nominated for election as Fall 2025 - Fall 2027 PhD rep!

If you'd like to be put up for election, please respond to this email by
Tuesday and confirm that you'll be around through Spring 2027. If you'd
like, you can also include a paragraph-length blurb about yourself that
we'll put on the (Google form) ballot.

We can also answer any questions about the position! Feel free to email.

Cheers,
Siyan & Tao.

emails.subject: No Subject
emails.body:
Hi Karthik, I went back to ask chatgpt to produce the code in Py Look at the graph attached. I was able to get that. But if you look at the upper left corner, there are overlapping For some reason, ChatGPT cannot fix that. They keep telling me "do this" or "do that" many, many times for hours. I've been on it since 5 a.m. this morning, and they can't get it right. They keep saying "oh, I see what the problem is" and "let me fix it" and run this, I do it, and it's the same thing. So then I ask them if they can at least go back because I asked him to lock the report, their command that produces this, at least so I can always come back to it. But now they can't even come back to this. I don't know why. What do you suggest I do? Please thank you. #!/usr/bin/env python3 """ FWK Locked Report Generator — Master Template v1.9_NameOnGraph Purpose: Generate one chart per player from an FWK JSON log, using the locked layout: - One graph per row - X-axis starts at 20:00 EST with 20-minute ticks - Y-axis 300–1500 with tick step 50 - Each player starts at 1000 - "Most" box ~above the highest point; "Lowest" box below the lowest point - Final score on the right (white box). Name shown italic red bottom-left - EST time display, clean layout, no overlaps for the two callouts Usage: python3 fwk_report_locked_v1.9.py "FWK - 10-10-2025.json" --outdir fwk_out_10_10 Notes: - This script is defensive about the JSON schema and tries common field variations. It should run against your nightly files without edits. - The script outputs a single HTML file with inline SVGs for portability. """ import argparse import base64 import io import json import math import os from collections import defaultdict from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone try: from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo # Python 3.9+ except Exception: # pragma: no cover ZoneInfo = None import matplotlib matplotlib.use("Agg") import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import matplotlib.dates as mdates # ----------------------------- Constants ------------------------------------ EST_TZ = ZoneInfo("America/New_York") if ZoneInfo else timezone(timedelta(hours=-5)) Y_MIN = 300 Y_MAX = 1500 Y_STEP = 50 START_POINTS = 1000 # Text / style ANN_FS = 9 NAME_COLOR = "red" NAME_STYLE = "italic" MOST_COLOR = "black" LOWEST_COLOR = "red" BOX_FC = "white" ARROW_LW = 1.3 ARROW_MS = 9 # Offsets (in display points) MOST_YOFF = 18 # above LOWEST_YOFF = -18 # below NAME_XOFF = 6 NAME_YOFF = 6 FINAL_BOX_XOFF = 14 # Clamp for annotation horizontal offsets so they stay inside frame CLAMP_X = 80 # ----------------------------- Helpers -------------------------------------- def parse_dt_est(s): """Parse an ISO-like datetime string as UTC, then convert to EST. Accepts strings like '2025-10-10T00:12:34Z' or without 'Z'. """ if not s: return None s = str(s).strip() # Normalize trailing Z if s.endswith("Z"): s = s[:-1] # Try a few formats fmts = [ "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%f", "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S", "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M", "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M", ] dt = None for f in fmts: try: dt = datetime.strptime(s, f).replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) break except Exception: pass if dt is None: # Last-ditch: maybe it's already local-ish; treat as UTC try: dt = datetime.fromisoformat(s) if dt.tzinfo is None: dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) except Exception: return None return dt.astimezone(EST_TZ) def smart_game_end(g): """Pick game end, fall back to start.""" return g.get("gameEndUtc") or g.get("game_end_utc") or g.get("endTimeUtc") or \ g.get("gameStartUtc") or g.get("game_start_utc") or g.get("startTimeUtc") def pick_player_name(p): for k in ("firstName", "name", "playerName", "displayName", "userName"): v = p.get(k) if v: # First name only (drop initials) v = str(v).strip() # If name has an initial like "David S." drop the initial parts = v.split() if parts: return parts[0] return v # Last resort: id-ish return str(p.get("playerId") or p.get("userId") or "Player") def pick_player_total(p): """Return the total points after this hand for a player. Tries several likely keys. If not found, falls back to delta accumulation. """ for k in ("totalPoints", "pointsAtEnd", "points_after_hand", "points", "score", "total", "pointsTotal", "endPoints"): if k in p: try: return float(p[k]) except Exception: pass # If only delta is present, return None and we'll accumulate outside return None def pick_player_delta(p): for k in ("delta", "diff", "change", "pointsChange", "pointsDelta"): if k in p: try: return float(p[k]) except Exception: pass return None def to_minutes(dt): return dt.replace(second=0, microsecond=0) # ----------------------------- Core ----------------------------------------- def load_series(path): with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f: data = json.load(f) games = data.get("games", []) # Materialize end time in EST enriched = [] for g in games: end_raw = smart_game_end(g) dt = parse_dt_est(end_raw) if not dt: continue g = dict(g) g["_end_est"] = dt enriched.append(g) # Sort by end time enriched.sort(key=lambda x: x["_end_est"]) # Build per-player series series_values = defaultdict(list) # name -> list of (t, value) # Track whether we must accumulate deltas totals_present = False any_deltas = False # First pass: detect availability for g in enriched: for p in g.get("players", []): if pick_player_total(p) is not None: totals_present = True if pick_player_delta(p) is not None: any_deltas = True # Accumulators if needed accum = defaultdict(lambda: START_POINTS) for g in enriched: t = g["_end_est"] for p in g.get("players", []): name = pick_player_name(p) v_total = pick_player_total(p) if v_total is None: # Use delta accumulation if available d = pick_player_delta(p) if d is None: # No info, skip this point for this player continue accum[name] += d v = accum[name] else: v = float(v_total) accum[name] = v # keep in sync series_values[name].append((t, v)) # Ensure everyone starts at START_POINTS at the first timestamp # Determine global first time rounded down to start-of-session floor of 20:00 if enriched: first_dt = enriched[0]["_end_est"].replace(second=0, microsecond=0) session_start = first_dt.replace(hour=20, minute=0) # If games started after midnight, keep session_start logical if first_dt < session_start: session_start -= timedelta(days=1) else: session_start = datetime.now(tz=EST_TZ).replace(hour=20, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0) for name, pts in series_values.items(): if not pts: continue pts.sort(key=lambda x: x[0]) # Prepend the starting point exactly at session_start if earlier than first event if pts[0][0] > session_start: pts.insert(0, (session_start, START_POINTS)) # If first value isn't START_POINTS, align visually by inserting a synthetic start elif pts[0][1] != START_POINTS: pts.insert(0, (session_start, START_POINTS)) return series_values, session_start def render_player(ax, name, pts, session_start): # Prepare x,y lists times = [t for (t, _) in pts] vals = [v for (_, v) in pts] # Plot line ax.plot(times, vals, linewidth=1.6) # Axes formatting ax.set_ylim(Y_MIN, Y_MAX) ax.set_yticks(list(range(Y_MIN, Y_MAX + 1, Y_STEP))) # X-axis range: from 20:00 that day to last event t0 = session_start t1 = max(times) if times else session_start + timedelta(hours=2) ax.set_xlim(t0, t1 + timedelta(minutes=5)) # 20-minute ticks locator = mdates.MinuteLocator(interval=20, tz=EST_TZ) formatter = mdates.DateFormatter("%H:%M", tz=EST_TZ) ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(locator) ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(formatter) # Clean spines for spine in ("top", "right"): ax.spines[spine].set_visible(False) # Player name, italic red, bottom-left inside plot ax.text(0.01, 0.02, name, transform=ax.transAxes, color=NAME_COLOR, style=NAME_STYLE, fontsize=11, ha="left", va="bottom") if not vals: return # Most (highest) and Lowest vmax = max(vals) vmin = min(vals) i_hi = vals.index(vmax) i_lo = vals.index(vmin) t_hi, v_hi = times[i_hi], vals[i_hi] t_lo, v_lo = times[i_lo], vals[i_lo] # Horizontal nudge to keep labels away from edges def clamp(n): return max(-CLAMP_X, min(CLAMP_X, int(n))) # Annotate Most (above) ax.annotate( "Most\n{}".format(int(round(vmax))), xy=(t_hi, v_hi), xytext=(clamp(12), MOST_YOFF), textcoords="offset points", ha="center", fontsize=ANN_FS, color=MOST_COLOR, bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.3", fc=BOX_FC, alpha=0.95), arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", lw=ARROW_LW, color=MOST_COLOR, mutation_scale=ARROW_MS) ) # Annotate Lowest (below) ax.annotate( "Lowest\n{}".format(int(round(vmin))), xy=(t_lo, v_lo), xytext=(clamp(-12), LOWEST_YOFF), textcoords="offset points", ha="center", fontsize=ANN_FS, color=LOWEST_COLOR, bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.3", fc=BOX_FC, alpha=0.95), arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", lw=ARROW_LW, color=LOWEST_COLOR, mutation_scale=ARROW_MS) ) # Final score box on right t_last, v_last = times[-1], vals[-1] ax.annotate( f"{int(round(v_last))}", xy=(t_last, v_last), xytext=(FINAL_BOX_XOFF, 0), textcoords="offset points", fontsize=9, bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.25", fc=BOX_FC, alpha=0.95), va="center" ) def fig_to_svg_data(fig): buf = io.BytesIO() fig.savefig(buf, format="svg", bbox_inches="tight") svg = buf.getvalue().decode("utf-8") buf.close() return svg def generate_html(outdir, series, session_start): os.makedirs(outdir, exist_ok=True) svgs = [] # Render one figure per player, one per row for name in sorted(series.keys()): pts = series[name] if not pts: continue fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9.5, 3.8)) render_player(ax, name, pts, session_start) svg = fig_to_svg_data(fig) plt.close(fig) svgs.append((name, svg)) # Simple self-contained HTML parts = [ "", "FWK Report v1.9_NameOnGraph", "", "", f"
Session start (EST): {session_start.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')}
", ] for name, svg in svgs: parts.append("
") parts.append(svg) parts.append("
") parts.append("") html = "\n".join(parts) html_path = os.path.join(outdir, "FWK_Report_v1.9_NameOnGraph.html") with open(html_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f: f.write(html) return html_path def main(): ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="FWK Locked Report Generator v1.9_NameOnGraph") ap.add_argument("json_path", help="Input FWK JSON file") ap.add_argument("--outdir", default="fwk_out", help="Output directory") args = ap.parse_args() series, session_start = load_series(args.json_path) if not series: raise SystemExit("No playable data found in JSON. Check file structure.") out = generate_html(args.outdir, series, session_start) print(out) if __name__ == "__main__": main()

emails.subject: guidelines from my CC section, and how to connect to hundreds of millions of academic articles
emails.body:
I paste below the guidelines I gave my class

But here's how you can connect it to OpenAlex
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://github.com/nac-codes/open-alex-mcp/blob/main/README.md__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WAdqjTk_7howxz0xlEl3aLk1ZEL7_Bd76DAkBt9rxWf_9uSxdSR9VThJXE54B0-E5C1mJXt8cqhLGoii-otwbxs$ > with
either Claude or ChatGPT (I think you will need a non-free account)

You can ignore the below, but extra credit for whoever come sup with the
best way to hack my efforts to AI-proof writing.

Matt

Valid uses of Ai include searching for sources, translating foreign
language materials, or analyzing technical aspects of your topic that might
otherwise be hard to understand. CUIT
<https://www.cuit.columbia.edu/services#!#%2Fservice%2Fterm%2F183 > now
makes several gen AI tools available for free (or almost free.) I
particularly recommend NotebookLM, since it will only generate answers from
the sources you load into your notebooks. If you use Gemini or CU CHAT (the
Columbia interface for ChatGPT), I recommend that you engineer your prompts
to focus on scholarly literature, provide sources, and double check all
work. If you have a Claude pro account you can connect it to OpenAlex
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://github.com/nac-codes/open-alex-mcp/blob/main/README.md__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WAdqjTk_7howxz0xlEl3aLk1ZEL7_Bd76DAkBt9rxWf_9uSxdSR9VThJXE54B0-E5C1mJXt8cqhLGoii-otwbxs$ >, which
will tap into a repository of hundreds of millions of academic articles.

But no matter what AI tools you use, do not depend on AI to summarize
anything you have not read for yourself. It will misrepresent sources and
invent them outright, in ways that will be invisible to you if you have not
done the work yourself. This can happen even when it produces links to the
sources it used. If you do not double check everything for yourself, the
resulting errors will be your responsibility, and they will seriously
reduce your grade. If it appears you have used AI to substitute for your
own critical reasoning, it will make me skeptical of everything else you
have written.

You may not use AI for any aspect of writing the paper, whether
brain-storming, outlining, drafting, critiquing, or editing. You need to do
all your writing in the same Google Doc. But you should insert document
tabs to separate the different components (topic proposal, lit review,
etc.) You can insert as many tabs as you want, such as to start a new draft
in the same document. Just be sure that you do all your writing and editing
in the same document, and clearly indicate the final version of each
component in the name of the tab (topic proposal, lit review, full paper).

Grammarly

Grammarly is a form of AI, like the built-in spelling and grammar checkers
in Google Docs. All of these tools can be used to highlight spelling and
grammatical errors, which is encouraged. But you should not use them for
any other purpose, such as suggesting ways to improve on your own writing.
Writing is hard work. It is not supposed to be easy. Writing is thinking,
right down to the way you choose particular words to express your precise
meaning. Putting yourself in the position of the reader usually requires
that you think hard about how to make your ideas clear and compelling. You
will not learn how to do this more effectively if you come to depend on
Grammarly or other tools as a crutch.

For that reason, if you plan to use Grammarly, you must configure it
appropriately. So turn off suggestion categories in the Grammarly web
editor, browser extension, or sidebar of Google Docs, i.e. deselect Clarity,
Engagement, and Delivery, and select just Correctness. That way, Grammarly
will mostly flag errors of grammar, spelling, and punctuation, and not
suggest synonyms or rewrites. Even if suggestions pop up, use these tools
only as a highlighter for potential issues, and decide for yourself whether
and how to fix them in your own way (if indeed they need to be fixed).

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Matthew Connelly

Professor of History

Vice Dean for AI Initiatives

403 Fayerweather Hall

Department of History

Mail Code 2508

1180 Amsterdam Avenue

Columbia University

New York, NY 10027

(212) 854-4563

On Fri, Oct 10, 2025 at 12:44 PM Matthew J. Connelly <mjc96@columbia.edu>
wrote:

> Dear Friends:
>
> Lydia and I are looking forward to seeing you in the Studio this morning.
> Please note that, based on our experience last time, we decided to reverse
> the topic we will be exploring today. Instead of text and context, we are
> going to focus on "Finding Connections." I've pasted the relevant part of
> the syllabus below.
>
> Best
>
> Matt
>
> Session 3: Finding Connections (early- to mid-November 2025)
>
> Making meaningful connections between texts is one of the most powerful
> ways to deepen understanding and strengthen memory. Drawing links—across
> works, authors, disciplines, and traditions—can help us grasp abstract
> ideas more concretely, see recurring patterns more clearly, and appreciate
> nuance more fully. But our brains often miss these opportunities. Sometimes
> we forget what we've read, or focus too narrowly. Sometimes connections
> only emerge after repeated encounters or guided reflection.
>
> In this session, students will experiment with using AI to surface
> connections between core texts—primarily from Contemporary Civilization,
> but also from Literature Humanities, Art Humanities, and Music Humanities.
> They will try prompting AI to relate different texts by theme (e.g.,
> justice), by intellectual lineage (e.g., Plato and Augustine), by
> rhetorical style (e.g., prophetic, dialogic, polemical), or by formal or
> aesthetic strategies.
>
> Faculty will support students in critically evaluating AI-generated
> connections: Are they substantive or superficial? Surprising or strained?
> What interpretive assumptions are embedded in the link? Students will then
> practice refining their own prompts to surface more meaningful insights and
> reflect on how the process enhances or limits their own capacity for
> synthesis. Ultimately, this session asks: What does it mean to read in
> conversation—not just with a single author, but with a tradition? And how
> can AI, used wisely, help us become more attentive and imaginative readers?
>
> ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
>

emails.subject: REMINDER: PhD Students Connect today!
emails.body:
Hi all,

Friendly reminder that *PhD Students Connect* is today at *1 pm* in *Mudd
524*.

Registration isn't required, but you'll need your CUID. Lunch is
first-come, first-served!

We look forward to seeing you soon!

Sincerely,
Andy

On Thu, Oct 2, 2025 at 8:13 PM Andy Chae <ac4897@columbia.edu> wrote:

> Dear Columbia Engineering doctoral students, candidates and Bridge
> Scholars,
>
> Please see details below for the first *PhD Students Connect* of the
> academic year:
>
>    - *Date: *Friday, October 10th,
>    - *Time: *1-2 pm
>    - *Location: *Mudd 524
>    - *Registration: *No registration; first-come, first-served, but *CUID
>    required *and offer only good until supplies last! :)

emails.subject: Re: syllabus, and something we will be looking at during our first session
emails.body:
Dear Friends:

Lydia and I are looking forward to seeing you in the Studio this morning.
Please note that, based on our experience last time, we decided to reverse
the topic we will be exploring today. Instead of text and context, we are
going to focus on "Finding Connections." I've pasted the relevant part of
the syllabus below.

Best

Matt

Session 3: Finding Connections (early- to mid-November 2025)

Making meaningful connections between texts is one of the most powerful
ways to deepen understanding and strengthen memory. Drawing links—across
works, authors, disciplines, and traditions—can help us grasp abstract
ideas more concretely, see recurring patterns more clearly, and appreciate
nuance more fully. But our brains often miss these opportunities. Sometimes
we forget what we've read, or focus too narrowly. Sometimes connections
only emerge after repeated encounters or guided reflection.

In this session, students will experiment with using AI to surface
connections between core texts—primarily from Contemporary Civilization,
but also from Literature Humanities, Art Humanities, and Music Humanities.
They will try prompting AI to relate different texts by theme (e.g.,
justice), by intellectual lineage (e.g., Plato and Augustine), by
rhetorical style (e.g., prophetic, dialogic, polemical), or by formal or
aesthetic strategies.

Faculty will support students in critically evaluating AI-generated
connections: Are they substantive or superficial? Surprising or strained?
What interpretive assumptions are embedded in the link? Students will then
practice refining their own prompts to surface more meaningful insights and
reflect on how the process enhances or limits their own capacity for
synthesis. Ultimately, this session asks: What does it mean to read in
conversation—not just with a single author, but with a tradition? And how
can AI, used wisely, help us become more attentive and imaginative readers?

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Matthew Connelly

Professor of History

Vice Dean for AI Initiatives

403 Fayerweather Hall

Department of History

Mail Code 2508

1180 Amsterdam Avenue

Columbia University

New York, NY 10027

(212) 854-4563

wrote:

> This morning I'll be referring to the syllabus from my own section of CC.
> Please see attached.

> Also, we will look at some examples of CC midterm questions. Here's one
> below.
>
> Best
>
> Matt
>
> Identify the following text -- where it comes from and the significance of
> the passage within its argumentative context. Your answer should consist of
> one shortish paragraph (equivalent to no more than one side of one page in
> the blue book). Identify the passage by number. (13 pts.; 3 for
> identification, 10 for significance.)
>
> “Every art and every inquiry, and similarly every action and pursuit, is
> thought to aim at some good; and for this reason the good has rightly been
> declared to be that at which all things aim. But a certain difference is
> found among ends; some are activities, others are products apart from the
> activities that produce them. Where there are ends apart from the actions,
> it is the nature of the products to be better than the activities.”
>

emails.subject: Final key step before joining Prolific as an expert
emails.body:
Hello Karthik,

At Prolific, we are committed to maintaining a high standard of quality and trust across our platform. To maintain the integrity of our platform and ensure a reliable space for both participants and researchers, we need to take steps to verify the background of certain individuals who may be considered ‘experts’ in a particular area, before onboarding.

As part of this process, we’re requesting that such participants and prospective participants provide us with the following:

* A link to their LinkedIn profile

This will help us to verify that the information shared is correct in order to prevent fraud through the provision of false or misleading information.You can add the information requested by clicking on the button below, and read more about how we handle your data in our Participant Privacy Notice ( https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.notion.so/Participant-Privacy-Notice-6b6cb4f60a674d96a1433bff6412215b?pvs=21__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X8DUyBqcNMVaAubzLwH-4Um2InSw1ghnXk8UmocfoevwrGSHqpgo6PvVVbnKsKCUT3j7rAOtCTLTxG3q9abHDR9pfYXFLg$  ).

Tell us more about your experience ( https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://app.prolific.com/register/participant/waitlist/?campaign_code=C15394Q3&waitlist_id=68e5440e1b46ef76e39c6345__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X8DUyBqcNMVaAubzLwH-4Um2InSw1ghnXk8UmocfoevwrGSHqpgo6PvVVbnKsKCUT3j7rAOtCTLTxG3q9abHDR__V7ajsQ$  )Thanks,

The Prolific Team

Information you provide in response to our waitlist survey is used to enable us to identify individuals with certain skills or types of background for Prolific, and is processed in accordance with our Participant Privacy Notice ( https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://participant-marketing.prolific.com/e/c/eyJlIjoxNjc0NjgsImVtYWlsX2lkIjoiZXhhbXBsZSIsImhyZWYiOiJodHRwczovL3Byb2xpZmljLm5vdGlvbi5zaXRlL1BhcnRpY2lwYW50LVByaXZhY3ktTm90aWNlLTZiNmNiNGY2MGE2NzRkOTZhMTQzM2JmZjY0MTIyMTViIiwidCI6MTc0NzMwNjY4MX0/9000dd326fabe28edfd275b123ac4825a2b92403bda86eee800a0af22cb2af5a__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X8DUyBqcNMVaAubzLwH-4Um2InSw1ghnXk8UmocfoevwrGSHqpgo6PvVVbnKsKCUT3j7rAOtCTLTxG3q9abHDR-vG7ppgA$  ).

	Dear student,

                     We are reaching out with a final warning with regard to the SEAS Orientation Tutorials requirement, which is mandatory for all SEAS graduate students. 

                     At this time, our records indicate that you have either not begun the tutorials or have not yet successfully completed all of them.

                     We are kindly asking that you complete this SEAS requirement by Monday, October 13, 2025.

                     If you do not complete the Orientation Tutorials by this date, you will receive a registration hold that will prevent you from registering for future courses. Once placed, the hold will not be lifted until the tutorials have been completed.

                     The Orientation Tutorials are available on Courseworks via the following course link:https://courseworks2.columbia.edu/courses/161622 . Please note that this requirement is in addition to thepre-orientation tutorials <https://universitylife.columbia.edu/welcome-columbia-pre-orientation-tutorials > and the university-wideCommunity Citizenship Initiative <https://universitylife.columbia.edu/CCI > (CCI) mandated by the Office of University Life.

                     As previously noted, you must receive a perfect score (100%) on each tutorial/quiz in order for this requirement to be considered complete. You may re-take quizzes as needed until you achieve a perfect score. Kindly note that the Food Pantry quiz is optional but strongly recommended.

                     Let us know of any questions or concerns that you might have.

                     Sincerely,

                     SEAS Academics

Engineering Student Affairs

Office of Graduate Admissions | Columbia Engineering

                        Office of Graduate Admissions                      

Dear Karthik,

                      You are receiving this email because it appears that we have not received all of your final official transcripts and/or proof of degree conferral.

                      🔹 Deadline
                                                                     All final official transcripts must be received by Wednesday, October 15, 2025.                      

                      🔹 How to Submit
                                                                     Transcripts must be certified and securely sent directly to Columbia University, Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science (or finaldocs@columbia.edu) from your institution's Registrar's Office.

                        Students who have attended a college or university in China will be required to contact CHESICC to arrange submission of a "Verification Report of China Higher Education Student's Academic Transcript" AND a "Verification Report of China Higher Education Qualification Certificate" (for those earning a degree in China) to Columbia at finaldocs@columbia.edu.

                        Students who have attended a university that is not in China or the United States must submit an electronic transcript if your school offers secure online delivery to finaldocs@columbia.edu. If the university cannot send transcripts electronically via a secure, password-protected system, applicants must order a Course-by-Course Evaluation and International Credential Advantage Package to be sent electronically to finaldocs@columbia.edu from World Education Services (WES).

                      If you have questions about how to submit final official transcripts, please refer to our FAQ <https://www.gradengineering.columbia.edu/faq/admitted-students >. If you believe you have received this email in error, please email finaldocs@columbia.edu.

emails.subject: Fwd: SEAS-wide Support for GenAI account
emails.body:
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Dean Chang <deanchang@columbia.edu>
Date: Mon, Sep 15, 2025 at 3:12 PM
Subject: SEAS-wide Support for GenAI account
To: <SEASGRAD-ALL@lists.columbia.edu>

Dear SEAS Community,

To provide our community with access to the latest AI computing resources,
we are pleased to announce a one-year pilot service, effective this
semester (Fall 2025), offering access to an open-source AI platform,
LibreChat, to all members of SEAS.

CHAT is Columbia University’s customized deployment of LibreChat. This
CU-specific interface provides a secure and private environment for using
AI tools while maintaining compliance with university standards. The
back-end LLMs currently include OpenAI’s ChatGPT, with additional models
(Anthropic’s Claude, Google’s Gemini, and others) to be added in the future.

Its usage is billed per interaction rather than a flat subscription. During
the FY26 pilot period, SEAS will cover the cost of use with a daily limit
for all SEAS members, including faculty, staff, and students, as well as
other students enrolled in SEAS courses requiring the use of GenAI.
*The daily limit available to each user will be displayed upon login,
depending on their access level.*

*No Training on Conversations: Chats are not used to train any LLMs.*

*Get Started with CHAT:*

   -

   Visit: https://chat.cuit.columbia.edu/login 
   <https://chat.cuit.columbia.edu/login?utm_source=chatgpt.com >
   -

   Log in with your UNI
   -

   Explore support resources and documentation:
   https://www.cuit.columbia.edu/content/chat 
   <https://www.cuit.columbia.edu/content/chat?utm_source=chatgpt.com >
   <https://www.cuit.columbia.edu/content/chat?utm_source=chatgpt.com >

We are committed to integrating the latest technology and resources into
our educational and research endeavors. Our faculty are at the frontier of
advancing AI. We have also been working diligently to enhance our
curriculum and teaching with AI (see a report on AI-enhanced SEAS courses
<https://www.engineering.columbia.edu/about/news/ai-enhanced-education >and
a report on CS programming courses
<https://www.engineering.columbia.edu/about/news/adapting-computer-science-education-changing-tech-landscape >
).

We look forward to seeing continued innovations in education and research
in this exciting time.

Shih-Fu Chang, Dean
Vishal Misra, Vice Dean of AI and Computing

[image: A view looking over Alma Mater’s shoulders across campus to Butler
Library.]

*October 6, 2025*

Dear Students and Faculty,

As you are aware, the University and the Student Workers of Columbia union
(SWC) have made little progress in negotiating a successor contract since
our first and only bargaining session in March. Information on our most
recent bargaining attempt
<https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=341c47e64254658159304d035a980065daca9c4bba43f394e39c4e741337bf5855b8ff4fb976d0f34fb6b8ae5b7f485fdce22f04b1ed8eb3c51574ae4cac4532 >
is available. Still, the University remains fully committed to supporting
our graduate student community and student employees.

Columbia has some of the finest young scholars in the world, who are
focused on their coursework, teaching, and research, and who contribute
much to making Columbia the great institution that it is. Accordingly,
although the University continues to navigate difficult financial headwinds
that will persist for the foreseeable future, Columbia *will implement the
following increases to student support, applied pro rata effective October
16, 2025*:

   - *Compensation will increase by 3%* for PhD student employees who hold
   a teaching or research appointment in a guaranteed year of support in GSAS,
   SEAS, the VP&S Coordinated Doctoral Programs in Biomedical Sciences, the
   School of Nursing, the Columbia Business School, the Mailman School of
   Public Health, GSAPP, Journalism, SIPA, and the School of Social Work. This
   increase will apply to the pay period beginning October 16, which is paid
   on November 7. PhD students receiving stipends at an on-appointment rate,
   including those beyond their guaranteed years of funding who have been
   released from teaching duties, will also receive 3% increases. Stipends
   payments will be issued the week of October 27, reflecting the increase for
   fall, prorated from the October 16 effective date.

   - PhD students not on appointment in guaranteed years of funding *will
   also receive 3% increases* to their minimum support stipends. Stipends
   payments will be issued the week of October 27, reflecting the increase for
   fall, prorated from the October 16 effective date.

   - In each academic unit, compensation * will increase by 3%* for
   undergraduate students, Master’s students, non-PhD doctoral students, and
   PhD students beyond their guaranteed years of support who hold appointments
   as Teaching Assistants, Teaching Fellows, Preceptors, Readers, Teaching
   Assistants III, Graduate Research Assistants and Department Research
   Assistants. This increase will apply to the pay period beginning October
   16, which is paid on November 7.

   - The University will also *increase the minimum hourly rate* for
   student employees who perform instructional or research work from
   $22.50/hour to $23.00/hour, starting October 20, 2025.

With the academic year now well underway, the University is disappointed
that the union has not returned to the bargaining table or responded to the
University’s proposals. Most recently, after canceling an agreed-upon
bargaining session on September 11, without explanation, SWC refused to
bargain at a scheduled meeting today, October 6. The union has posted that
the University “canceled” today’s session. The University’s bargaining team
was present at the neutral Interchurch location from the agreed-upon start
time and remained ready to bargain. SWC’s delegation arrived an hour late
to present demands but would not stay to negotiate or discuss scheduling
future sessions, asking instead for email replies. The full update on the
scheduled October 6 bargaining session
<https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=341c47e6425465814316b759a22f869e97d1b139590debad1e8ebc5f35410a188bb4da170daae4caa9f162fb6214bce74f3b305ba8c5daf70a35bd3a8b123f33 >
is available.

Additional information regarding negotiations is available under the Current
Negotiations
<https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=341c47e6425465819237c7b8c46a9e99e2d0d51f24b4c38df11640c6adce8c9c2cdec81af891f1970c10e3f9309fc9b9525c7c4616b44f4e3735eba1596596a5 >
tab on the Student Benefits website.

Thank you for everything you do to support our community and advance
Columbia’s teaching and research mission.

Sincerely,

Amy Hungerford
Dean and Executive Vice President, Faculty of Arts and Sciences

Cas Holloway
Special Advisor to Acting President Claire Shipman

*Copyright © 2025 Columbia University. All rights reserved.*

You are receiving this email because you are part of the Columbia
University community.

*Columbia University - Office of Public Affairs*
402 Low Library, MC 4321, 535 W. 116th St
New York, NY 10027

*Confidentiality Notice:*

*The information contained in the email message is intended only for the
personal and confidential use of the recipient(s) named above.  If the
reader of this message is not the intended recipient or an agent
responsible for delivering it to the intended recipient, you are hereby
notified that you have received this document in error and that any review,
dissemination, or copying of this message is strictly prohibited.  If you
have received this communication in error, please notify me immediately by
email, and delete the original message.*

Dear residents, 

NYC is expecting inclement weather tomorrow, October 30, with rainfall anticipated to reach up to one inch and wind gusts up to 20-30mph. 

In preparation for the weather, and to ensure everyone’s safety, please remember to keep all windows shut, and move any outdoor furniture indoors and off of terraces, patios, and roof areas.  

If you experience any issues, please don't hesitate to reach out to your management team. 

Please also note the emergency line that can be reached anytime at (212) 518- 2690 ext 111. Our support center is also available at all times at support@md2pg.com. 

Thank you for your attention, enjoy your weekend, and please let us know if you have any questions or need anything.

Best regards,
MD2 Property Group

emails.subject: Thank you for your Application!
emails.body:
Hi, Karthik, 

Thank you for applying to the Research Intern - AI Agents (Job number:1903410) position at Microsoft!  We're glad you're interested in a career at Microsoft and we're here to help you find a perfect fit.
 
You may not receive feedback from us on your application directly, but please know that it’s being evaluated, and you’ll hear from us as soon as the review process is complete. If you’re selected for an interview, you’ll be notified by someone on the recruiting team.
 
You can view your application status updates through your Action Center. If you see the job moved to an Archived state, that means the position is either no longer open, you withdrew from consideration, or you were not selected for the role.
 
You may notice that we move your application from one role to another. This may happen a few times and is a normal part of our recruiting process. So, if you see the job you applied to in an Archived state, and a new job listed as Active, please know that this is normal and does not negatively impact your candidacy in any way.
 
How’s your profile?
A key part of the review process is evaluating your profile in relation to the job requirements, so please make sure your profile is accurate and extensive – it’s our first step in getting to know you! You can build your profile anyway you’d like – you can import it from LinkedIn, manually update it, or import/attach a resume. The most important thing is that your profile tells your story!
 
We encourage you to check back frequently and continue to look for opportunities that match your interests, as new jobs are being posted regularly.
 
Thank you,
Microsoft Recruiting

This mail is sent from an unmonitored mailbox. Please do not reply. 
Microsoft respects your privacy. To learn more, please read our Microsoft Data Privacy Notice.

**************************************************

emails.subject: Happy Halloweek 🎃🕸️👻 | EGSC Weekly Newsletter
emails.body:
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/  >
*Hey Grad SEAS!*
Spooky season is here, and we’re celebrating this *Friday *at our* EGSC
Halloween Social*! Join us for festive *treats*, spooky *activities*, and
hauntingly *good vibes* with your fellow grad students 🎃👻

Don't forget to *follow us on Instagram @columbiaegsc
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.instagram.com/columbiaegsc/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg9getv3k$ > *to stay updated on all our
events!

*👻 Halloween Social 🎃*
 Join us for a spook-tacular afternoon of fall festivities!
📆 *Date: *October 31
⏰ *Time: *12:00 PM – 3:00 PM
📍 *Location:* Back of Carleton Commons
Come build a haunted Lego masterpiece, decorate your own pumpkin, and enjoy
cozy treats like hot cider, hot chocolate, and pie crust cookies.
Take a break from debugging, and start *de-BOO-gging* instead!! 🕸️🧡
Join us at our *weekly GBMs*, whether you are a Department Representative
or not! We meet on Fridays from 4-5 pm in Mudd 524.

This week, we will be joined by *CPS* and *Stan Sakry, Senior Director of
Facilities and Space Planning*!

*Lunch & Learn: LGBTQ+ History Month Edition*
Come grab lunch and celebrate LGBTQ+ History Month with us! While you eat,
enjoy a fun slideshow of surprising facts, trailblazing figures, and
milestones from LGBTQ+ history!

*Join the ELC Executive Board 2026!*
The Entrepreneurship Leadership & Consulting (ELC) Club is one of Columbia
Engineering’s oldest and most dynamic student organizations—connecting
aspiring entrepreneurs and future consultants through hands-on events,
speaker series, and startup collaborations.

They're looking for passionate students to join our 2026 Executive Board!
Open positions include VP Entrepreneurship, VP Management Consulting, VP
Tech Consulting, VP Operations, and VP External Affairs.

📅 Deadline: *October 31st, 11:59 PM*
🔗 Apply Here
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://forms.gle/xrtSTVBt23YSbwby7__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!XlQ6LxSGX7Asj52kkRQ_41EAnyPCGA9MeJiJeWIarXme4l0cxxsMQUAXB5EFTeuSzUEw6cb9ZQ7ZOp9jz4CTqfVTAkh6Tx2LSAKquFQ$>
Come join the *Columbia Entrepreneurship & Venture Society* for their *General
Body Meeting* this *Wednesday* at *11:00 a.m.* in *Uris 326*! They'll be
discussing upcoming events and ways to get involved in the club. Whether
you’re a returning member or new to CEVS, they'd love to see you there.
Meetings are held *every other Wednesday*, so don’t miss out on the chance
to connect, share ideas, and grow your network!

*The First-Ever Columbia Product Hackathon 2025 is here!* 🚀

Hosted by the *Columbia Product Managers Club (PMC)* in collaboration with
the *NYU Product Management Club (NYU PMC)* — and joined by *Wharton,
Cornell, Duke, Northeastern University, Boston University, Carnegie Mellon
University (CMU), University of Southern California (USC), and UC Berkeley* —
this event brings together the brightest product minds from top schools for
an epic, cross-campus innovation challenge.

Over *48 hours*, teams will *think, build, and pitch* product solutions to
real-world problems.

📅 *Event Timeline*
💻 *Nov 3:* Online Kickoff & Prompt Release
🏫 *Nov 4:* In-Classroom Round @ Columbia (Mudd 1127) at *6 PM*
🏁 *Nov 6:* National Semi-Finals @ NYU Campus
🏆 *Nov 9:* National Finals @ NYU Campus

🕓 *Application Deadline: Oct 29, 2025 (EOD)* — don’t miss your shot to
represent Columbia!

💰
*$3,000+ Prize Pool🎯 Mentorship *from top PM leaders in tech & consulting
*📜 Certificates, swag, and national recognition at NYU*

📝 *Sign up now — we’ll share more details after you register!*
Google Form Link:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdOB91NpIxAMv0nuJbi6CjF-mvPQ1GPKI8rcYBtFbJ6MvDzYA/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgAShtI5Q$ 
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdOB91NpIxAMv0nuJbi6CjF-mvPQ1GPKI8rcYBtFbJ6MvDzYA/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!VQBWTHLuV9S-vvKJg04JMYFEtMFDjEwDLWHH5dnXQpYDFZ1Y3qJ7GAUsU1KhqNBknk2U4G5JzcXoEZmfKokT1B4nfEfsXCfxorkgZ2Y$>
or scan the *QR code* on the poster.

📩 More details and team confirmations will be shared via email after
registration.
⚠️ *Registrations close October 29, EOD*.

💡 Let’s make history, Lions — *build, innovate, and lead the future of
product!* 🦁🔥

*Upcoming Columbia Business School Events!*

   - Weds, Nov 5, 6-7pm. Lang Talk with Cyrus Massoumi, founder of
Zocdoc. *RSVP
   here. <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/ia6dvjbz__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgv2bokeg$ >*
   - Weds, Nov 12, 6-8:30pm. CBS Big Idea Pitch Competition and Networking
   event. *RSVP link pending.*
   - Thurs, Nov 13, 12:30-2:30pm. CBS Tabling: Taste & Shop at CBS. *RSVP
   here. <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/nvrzzvpv__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgN9vDTCc$ >*
   - Fri, Nov 14, 10-1:30pm*. *The AI Advantage: Building and Funding the
   Next Wave of Startups. *RSVP here. <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/7nwiifge__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgCzFTlUM$ >*

*The  <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >**Spring
2026 CBL Engineering application
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >** is now open!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >
*Columbia Build Lab (CBL)
<https://business.columbia.edu/lang/student-and-alumni-programming/launch/columbia-build-lab  >*
matches
Columbia students pursuing Computer Science (or related degrees) with CBS
student-founders of an early-stage startup. Engineers in the Build Lab
program gain experience working to solve real-world challenges with teams
of peers and CBS founders. This is an excellent opportunity for any
engineering or computer science student looking to gain valuable experience
over the summer!
Submit your application to the Spring 2026 cohort by December 7!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >
Interested in joining *Columbia’s only student club dedicated to
Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI)*? At *Neurotech X Columbia*, you’ll find
opportunities to engage in cutting-edge research projects  and connect with
peers passionate about neurotechnology. We welcome all members of the
Columbia community to join us — please register through this Google Form:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/1r6jLNyCo6pbxXR3jEqPT0a_FzcpqlB4d4iCH3_75ZbQ__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgSbHABoA$ 
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/1r6jLNyCo6pbxXR3jEqPT0a_FzcpqlB4d4iCH3_75ZbQ__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!U66AXcO3CK09cjy5CEinktcs0lyktt1ow3JxvVucDXBZcW_LeYTqdaFHhq0nJeCzhWoF8A1KI_e8L7oaewiVfhUko8lKWg$>

Thanks for applying to Google! There are a ton of great companies out

emails.subject: Thanks for accepting Kavya Sreedhar’s invitation to apply to Google
emails.body:
Google Careers

Time to find the perfect role at Google

Hi Karthik Sreedhar,

We're thrilled that you've accepted Kavya's invitation to apply to Google!  
You have 30 days to apply to up to 3 jobs with this invitation, which also  
enables Kavya to receive updates on the status of your application(s). Make  
sure to apply using the invitation link and the same email address where  
you received the invitation. If you already applied within the last 30 days  
you don't have to apply again. However, your referrer may not receive  
updates on those applications. Remember, if you want to apply to additional  
roles once this invitation has expired, be sure to ask Kavya to send you a  
new invitation.

EXPLORE OPEN ROLES

Looking forward to seeing your application!

-The Google Staffing team

Info icon Need help finding the right role?

Kavya can always give you some pointers, but generally we recommend  
focusing first on roles where your skills match the minimum qualifications  
and some of the prefered qualifications, then tailoring your resume to  
highlight those skills. Learn more about how Google hires.

This email was sent to you as a transactional notification of activity  
related to Google Staffing.

Google logo
1600 Amphitheatre Parkway,
Mountain View, CA 94043, USA

You received this message because someone that works at Google referred you.

emails.subject: Invitation from Abhijit Adsule to apply to Google
emails.body:
Google Careers logo

Build for everyone

Abhijit wants to work with you at Google

Hi KARTHIK SREEDHAR,

Abhijit has invited you to apply for a job at Google! Start the application  
process by viewing their invitation. By accepting their invitation, you'll  
allow us to share high level application status updates with this Googler.  
They will see the next 1-3 roles you apply to in the coming 30 days, as  
well as any roles you've applied to in the past 30 days where the  
application is still open. If you'd like Abhijit to be kept in the loop, be  
sure to accept and apply through the link using your ks4190@columbia.edu  
email address! If you want to later apply to more roles, make sure to ask  
them to send you a new invitation. You may also decline the invitation if  
you'd prefer that status updates not be shared with Abhijit.

The link to accept is only active for the next 30 days, so accept it soon.  
Then, follow the “Search jobs” link to learn more about open Google roles  
and apply. If you've applied to roles in the last 30 days, you don't have  
to apply again.

Make sure to use this invitation link so we know you've been referred.

VIEW INVITATION TO APPLY

We hope to hear from you soon,

The Google Staffing Team

Please note, any information you enter during the application and  
recruitment process will be kept confidential in compliance with Google's  
Candidate Privacy Policy.

Google logo

© 2021 Google LLC 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

You received this message because someone that works at Google referred you.

emails.body:
[image: unnamed.png]

Dear Computer Scientist,

Mercor Intelligence is hiring for a variety of projects to start this
winter and in 2026. We have part-time remote positions available in
computer science, AI, and data science. Our AI company assists major
artificial intelligence and social media companies in San Francisco and
Silicon Valley. We recruit computer scientists around the nation and around
the world. To learn more about our positions available now, click the links
below.

*AI Red Teamer - Adversarial AI Advanced Testing *$54-$100 hourly,
Part-time or Full-time, Remote
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://work.mercor.com/jobs/list_AAABmX4FiBxRn-I1VM5KeZ2h?referralCode=f529623d-0b53-4f0a-9f1d-2a0d884c641f__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RL2VlSsAzD0ZQb6OWGNlRdDvuF9fpMDQxPBcwo6VCvLRqTr4zE7r7mDPRRJ1yUzQbJxBiZmpL5a4883BcU8IbRpxU6TL9Wyb$ 

*Data Science, AI Tutor* $45-$100 hourly, Full-time, Remote or at office in
Palo Alto
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://work.mercor.com/jobs/list_AAABmfXLudLUdLZDSaZBN687?referralCode=f529623d-0b53-4f0a-9f1d-2a0d884c641f__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RL2VlSsAzD0ZQb6OWGNlRdDvuF9fpMDQxPBcwo6VCvLRqTr4zE7r7mDPRRJ1yUzQbJxBiZmpL5a4883BcU8IbRpxU8hBZNtS$

Part-Time, Remote

We’d love for you to stay in the loop—please feel free to view the full job
description on our website at the link above. On the website, you can
submit your resume, complete a background check, and even take part in a

Best Wishes,

Rebecca Brudzynski 😀
